Reports To: Head of Sales



Job Overview:

As a Sales Consultant at itGenius, you'll be at the forefront of our mission to "Help small business play a bigger game," directly contributing to our vision of "facilitating happy, healthy, productive teams fuelling important global purpose-work." You embody the spirit of being the "Amazon Prime" of IT – highly responsive and providing solutions that are more convenient than Google or AI.

You'll operate like a member of a "high-performing sports team," demonstrating 10x thinking to achieve ambitious goals. As a "useful Geek," you'll leverage your love for technology to provide smart, human-centric solutions. This role is crucial in expanding our reach within the SMB market, acquiring new clients, and nurturing relationships that drive our growth towards becoming the global leader in remote-delivered IT services. You will be directly responsible for identifying opportunities, presenting our ingenious and seamless solutions, and ensuring our customers trust us to be an essential part of their success.





Core Accountabilities

The Sales Consultant will be held accountable for the following measurable objectives:

Individual Gross Profit Target Achievement: Consistently meet or exceed assigned quarterly and annual sales gross profit targets for new and existing clients

Sales Pipeline Health & Progression: Proactively manage and maintain a robust sales pipeline, ensuring a consistent flow of qualified leads and efficient progression of opportunities through all stages of the sales funnel.

Client Satisfaction & Smooth Onboarding: Ensure high levels of prospective client satisfaction throughout the sales process, culminating in a seamless and positive handover experience to the delivery and account management teams.





Key Responsibilities:

The day-to-day activities and duties of the Sales Consultant include:



Prospecting & Lead Qualification: Proactively identify, research, and qualify new sales opportunities within the small to medium business (SMB) market through various channels, including outbound prospecting, inbound lead follow-up, networking, and referrals.



Needs Assessment & Solution Design: Conduct in-depth needs assessments with prospective clients to thoroughly understand their business challenges, technology requirements, and strategic objectives.



Solution Presentation & Value Articulation: Develop and deliver compelling presentations and demonstrations of itGenius's tailored IT solutions and services, clearly articulating their value proposition, benefits, and return on investment.



Proposal Development & Negotiation: Prepare comprehensive and accurate sales proposals, quotes, and contracts. Lead negotiations to secure mutually beneficial agreements and close sales.



CRM Management & Reporting: Maintain accurate, up-to-date, and detailed records of all sales activities, client interactions, pipeline status, and forecasts within the Customer Relationship Management (CRM) system. Provide regular sales performance reports to the Head of Sales.



Cross-Functional Collaboration: Collaborate closely with the technical, operations, and delivery teams to ensure proposed solutions are feasible, client expectations are managed effectively, and client onboarding is seamless.

Market & Product Knowledge: Continuously stay informed about industry trends, competitor activities, emerging technologies, and itGenius's evolving product and service offerings to effectively position our solutions.



Post-Sales Handoff: Facilitate smooth and comprehensive handovers of new clients to the dedicated account management and service delivery teams, ensuring continuity and client satisfaction.

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Required Qualifications:

- Experience: 2-3+ years B2B sales experience (ideally IT services or SaaS)

- Track record of meeting/exceeding GP targets

- Experience with CRM tools (Pipedrive, HubSpot, Salesforce, etc.)

- Strong written and verbal communication

- Familiarity with Google Workspace / cloud IT would be a plus





Skills:

- Consultative/solution selling (needs assessment, value articulation)

- CRM pipeline management

- Proposal writing and negotiation

- Presentation and demo delivery

- SMB market awareness

- Basic IT/cloud technology literacy (enough to be a "useful Geek")

- Cross-functional collaboration



It would be nice if you have:

- Proven track record in Managed Service Provider (MSP) sales.

- AI Integration: Experience using AI (like GeniusAI) and automation to optimize workflows.



Work Schedule

8-hour shift

Philippine timezone, morning shift

Monday to Friday

Weekends off

Observance of Philippine holiday

Permanent Work Form Home



You’ll thrive here if you’re interested in:

The evolving world of cloud-based work (Google Workspace, Google Drive, Gmail, etc.)

Supporting small business growth (1–30 employees)

Applying technology to improve productivity and collaboration

title: Marketing Project Manager (Growth Ops)
description:
PLEASE READ: If you would like to apply and learn more about the role, please use the link below.





---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Mission: Helping Small Businesses Scale through Technology



At itGenius, we believe technology is the ultimate multiplier for growth. As a premier Google Workspace partner, we’ve spent 15 years helping over 5,000 business owners transition from startup chaos to scalable, efficient operations with Google Workspace and other cloud tools.



We are currently building a global, high-volume, systems-first consultancy. To achieve this, we need a Marketing Project Manager who views marketing as an engineering challenge—focusing on building the “pipes” that drive consistent, global growth.



The Role: Strategic Execution & Workflow Management

We prioritize data-driven execution over traditional marketing “fluff.”



Reporting directly to the Chief of Growth, your role is to bridge the gap between high-level strategy and tactical output. You won’t be managing a large internal team; instead, you will orchestrate a network of specialist contractors and automated systems to ensure our organic growth engine—primarily driven by our high-authority YouTube channel—is running at peak performance.



? Why Join Our High-Performance Team?

We offer a remote-first culture that values systems, clarity, and measurable results.



Work From Anywhere: Permanent work-from-home (aligned with Philippine Time).

Clear Career Growth: Budget for professional certifications and a defined path toward senior leadership.

Performance-Based Rewards: A monthly success bonus tied directly to the achievement of your KPIs.

Dynamic Culture: Annual team conferences (past locations include Boracay) and a focus on holistic well-being.

Comprehensive Benefits: Competitive compensation, health allowances, and tech support for your home office.





Core Responsibilities

You will oversee the marketing portfolio, ensuring every project is clearly defined, properly resourced, and delivered on schedule.



1. Content Strategy & Production Management: Direct our external editors and copywriters. Your task is to ensure all content aligns with our brand voice—the “Calm Expert Guide”—and effectively solves real-world business problems for our audience.

2. Growth Research & Competitive Analysis: Oversee our YouTube and search research process. You will identify high-demand topics and work with creatives to package them into high-performing titles and thumbnails.

3. Customer Lifecycle & Marketing Automation: Manage and optimize the customer journey within our CRM (Intercom). You will build and refine automated nurture sequences that guide prospects through our service tiers.

4. Technical SEO Coordination: Collaborate with our Engineering team to ensure our web properties remain healthy, crawlable, and optimized for the next generation of AI-driven search.

5. Performance Analytics: You will monitor and validate marketing KPIs bi-weekly, providing actionable insights during our Growth Sync meetings to improve ROI across all organic channels.





Who You Are (Fit & Mindset)

We look for candidates who demonstrate high ownership and a systems-first approach to problem-solving.



The “Systems Thinker”: You fundamentally understand B2B marketing funnels and how to manage complex workflows in a remote, international environment.

The “Tech-Savvy Professional”: You are comfortable navigating new tools, directing technical implementations, and taking absolute accountability for project outcomes.

The “Accountable Executor”: You are a power-user of project management tools (Asana preferred). You can write clear, “fail-proof” creative briefs and keep multiple contractors moving toward a single goal.





Required Qualifications

- 5+ Years in Marketing Operations or Project Management: Preferably within a B2B, SaaS, or tech consulting environment.

- Contractor Management: Proven experience sourcing, briefing, and holding external freelancers accountable for quality and deadlines.

- Tool Proficiency: Strong experience with Asana (or similar), CRM automation platforms, and an understanding of YouTube/Search analytics.

- Communication Excellence: You bring solutions, not just problems, and communicate with clarity and professionalism.





Work Schedule

- 8-hour shift, Philippine Time but flexible as some team are based in Europe.

- Monday to Friday (Weekends off).





Are You Ready to Scale with Us?

If you’re ready to build a scalable marketing machine that delivers tangible business impact, we’d love to meet you.



Apply Now and tell us: Which specific marketing system or workflow have you built that you are most proud of?

Competitive Compensation & Bonuses:

- December Bonus

- Performance Incentive Bonus

- Annual Salary Increases



Time Off That Matters:

- Paid Leaves

- Paid National/Local Holidays



Work & Life Experiences You'll Remember:

- Annual Team Conferences & Events (travel, food, accommodation covered)



Investing in Your Growth:

- Training & Development Budget

- Career Growth Pathways



Support to Help You Thrive:

- Government-Mandated Benefits/ HMO





Reports To: Head of Sales & Accounts



Job Overview: 



As a Senior Account Manager, you will be at the forefront of this mission, acting as a trusted partner and strategic advisor to our valued clients.



You will be instrumental in ensuring their continued success, identifying opportunities for them to leverage technology more effectively. This role is about building lasting relationships, driving mutual growth, and ensuring our clients experience the "Amazon Prime" of IT service - effortless, responsive and world class. 



You will be directly accountable for client retention, satisfaction, and the growth of your assigned accounts, contributing significantly to itGenius's 10-year goal of becoming the global leader in remote-delivered IT services to the SMB market.



Accountabilities



The Senior Account Manager is specifically accountable for the following measurable objectives within their assigned client portfolio:



Holding the Game Plan: Accountable for holding a clients long term objectives and ensuring the plan is constantly moved forward over time via quarterly reviews.



Strategic Client Partnership: Holding strategic relationships with key decision-makers within assigned client accounts, positioning itGenius as a trusted technology partner.



Client Retention & Satisfaction: Accountable for maintaining high levels of client satisfaction and ensuring the retention of assigned client accounts.



Revenue Growth: Accountable for identifying and securing opportunities for revenue growth through upsell and cross-sell within their assigned client portfolio.





Key Responsibilities:



To achieve the above accountabilities, the Senior Account Manager will perform the following day-to-day activities:



Relationship Management: Proactively engage with assigned clients through regular meetings (virtual and in-person where appropriate), calls, and
 communications to understand their evolving business needs and technology landscape.



Needs Assessment & Solution Proposal: Conduct thorough needs analyses to identify client challenges and opportunities, and present tailored IT solutions and services that align with their strategic objectives and our service offerings.



Sales & Negotiation: Prepare and deliver proposals, negotiate contracts, and manage the sales cycle for upsell and cross-sell opportunities within existing accounts to achieve revenue targets.



Client Advocacy & Issue Resolution: Act as the primary point of contact for assigned clients, advocating for their needs internally and collaborating with the technical, support, and project teams to ensure timely and effective resolution of any issues or escalations.



Account Planning & Review: Develop and maintain comprehensive account plans for each assigned client, including key contacts, service history, future opportunities, and conduct regular business reviews to demonstrate value and align on future strategies.



CRM Management & Reporting: Maintain accurate and up-to-date client information, communication logs, and sales pipeline data within the CRM system. Prepare and present regular reports on account health, sales performance, and client satisfaction to the Head of Strategic Sales.



Tea
ntoring: Serve as a subject matter expert and mentor to Junior Account Managers and Sales Representatives, assisting in their professional development and contributing to team best practices.



Market Awareness: Stay informed about industry trends, technological advancements, and the competitive landscape to better advise clients and identify new opportunities.





Required Qualifications & Skills



Bachelor’s degree in Business, Marketing, IT, or related field

5–8+ years in account management, sales, or client services, ideally in IT/technology/SaaS. 

Proven track record in client retention, revenue growth, and account expansion.

Client Relationship & Strategy: Build trusted partnerships with senior decision-makers. Develop account plans, conduct business reviews, and identify growth opportunities.

Sales & Solution Selling: Skilled in proposal creation, contract negotiation, upsell/cross-sell, and delivering tailored IT solutions.

Communication & Collaboration: Excellent verbal and written skills; adept at presenting to executives and working seamlessly with internal teams.

CRM & Reporting: Proficient in CRM tools for tracking accounts, pipeline management, and reporting on KPIs.

Leadership & Mentoring: Mentor junior account managers and contribute to process improvements.

Behavioral Competencies: Proactive, innovative, accountable, collaborative, and trusted.





Work Schedule



8-hour shift

Morning shift

Monday to Friday

Weekends off

Observance of Philippine holiday

Permanent Work Form Home





You’ll thrive here if you’re interested in:



The evolving world of cloud-based work (Google Workspace, Google Drive, Gmail, etc.)

Supporting small business growth (1–30 employees)

Applying technology to improve productivity and collaboration

title: QuickBooks Accountant Needed – Cleanup + Ongoing (Vacation & Car Rental Business)
description:
We’re looking for an experienced QuickBooks accountant to clean up and reconstruct our 2024–2025 books using bank statements and Airbnb-style payout reports.

This is a high-priority project (10-day turnaround) as we are preparing financial statements for a bank loan application. Includes recording and/or verifying fixed assets (including property) and calculating appropriate depreciation entries 

After cleanup, this will transition into ongoing weekly work (2–5 hours/week), with potential for additional VA tasks related to our vacation rental business.

Must have strong QuickBooks experience and prior cleanup/reconstruction work. Experience preparing financial statements that include fixed assets and depreciation schedules (required for loan reporting). CPA preferred.

title: VIRTUAL ASSISTANT | Tech-Savvy & Systems-Driven
description:
VIRTUAL ASSISTANT Sew & Tell Co. | Remote | 5-10 hours/week



Hi, I'
gan, the founder of Sew & Tell Co. (
), an online sewing education business based in Melbourne, Australia. I create courses, run a membership community, and host a twice-yearly online event for thousands of students around the world.



I'm looking for a detail-oriented VA to join my business. This is a long-term role for the right person who takes pride in doing things right the first time, communicates proactively, and comes with solutions rather than just problems.



What you'll be doing:

- Scheduling
s into Kit (ConvertKit)

- Managing a customer support inbox with help from AI and a knowledge base

- Scheduling social media content across Instagram and

- Uploading YouTube videos (descriptions, chapters, thumbnails, end screens)

- Setting up ManyChat keyword automations

- Creating simple graphics in Canva using provided brand templates (a keen eye for brand consistency is a real bonus here)

- General ClickUp task management and admin



What I'm looking for: I have SOPs and training for every tool. What I can't train is attitude. I need someone who is systems-oriented, tech savvy and self-motivated; someone who owns their workload without being chased, flags problems early, and comes with solutions rather than just problems.



Tools we use: Kit, Canva, Meta Business Suite, YouTube Studio, ManyChat, ClickUp, Google Drive, Slack



Nice to have: WordPress, Elementor, ThriveCart, CapCut, StreamYard, Plannable



Hours: 5-10 hours per week to start, with room to grow Availability: Extended hours required twice a year during live online events (with plenty of notice)

1. What is your WhatsApp ID or number?

2. What tools on the list above have you used before? Which are new to you?

3. What is your expected hourly rate in USD?

4. What does a typical working day look like for you, and how do you communicate your progress to a client?

5. Visit https://www.speedtest.net and perform an internet speed test. Share your results for both upload and download speed.



Applications sent via DM will not be considered.

title: Finance & Office Manager
description:
About the Role

We are a fast-growing Australian-based accounting and financial services group seeking a capable Finance & Office Manager to manage internal finance and office operations across our group of businesses.



Our group operates across multiple divisions including bookkeeping, tax, finance broking, real estate, and global talent services. As the business continues to grow, we are looking for someone who can take ownership of iinternal operational matters, ensuring that the firm runs efficiently behind the scenes.



This role will manage the firm’s internal finance, office administration, compliance coordination, and practice management processes, allowing the leadership team to focus on client service and business growth.



The ideal candidate will have strong accounting capability, excellent organisational skills, and the ability to manage multiple internal functions simultaneously.



This position reports to the Operations Manager and works closely with internal teams to ensure smooth daily operations across the organisation. This role is expected to take ownership of all internal operational matters of the firm, including internal finance, office administration, compliance coordination, telecommunications management, marketing coordination, and practice management.



The Finance & Office Manager will work closely with the Operations Manager to ensure internal systems run efficiently and that the organisation operates in a structured and well-managed manner.



As the business continues to grow, the successful candidate will have the opportunity to build and manage internal processes and support functions, helping strengthen the operational backbone of the group.



Key Responsibilities

1. Internal Finance Management

Oversee internal financial administration across group entities, including:

• Bank reconciliations across group companies

• Accounts Payable and Accounts Receivable management

• Internal billing administration and monitoring

• Expense tracking and cost monitoring

• Payroll administration support and superannuation coordination

• Supporting end-of-month financial administration including revenue tracking, gross profit monitoring and expense allocation

• Inter-company cross charges and internal allocations

• Supporting internal financial reporting preparation

• Supporting quarterly compliance preparation (BAS, IAS and related reporting)



2. Office Management

Manage internal office operations and administrative systems to ensure smooth day-to-day business operations.

Responsibilities include:

• Office administration and operational coordination

• Telecommunications management (phone systems, internet services and providers)

• Office facilities and vendor coordination

• Office systems administration and operational procedures

• Supporting internal office team coordination

• Monitoring administrative workflow efficiency

• Maintaining organised operational documentation and records



3. Marketing & Internal Coordination

Support internal marketing and communication coordination.

This includes:

• Coordinating marketing administration and campaign activities

• Supporting internal marketing initiatives

• Maintaining marketing materials and documentation

• Coordinating with external marketing partners when required

• Supporting internal communication initiatives



4. Compliance & Practice Management

Support internal governance and operational compliance across the firm.

Responsibilities include:

• Maintaining internal compliance calendars

• Coordinating annual administration including insurance renewals, workers compensation administration and internal compliance tracking

• Supporting practice management systems and processes

• Ensuring internal documentation and procedures remain organised and up to date



Reporting Structure

This role reports to the Operations Manager and works closely with internal teams to manage the firm’s internal finance and office operations.

The Finance & Office Manager will play an important role in ensuring all internal matters of the firm are properly managed and organised, supporting the overall efficiency of the business.



Requirements

• Bachelor’s degree in Accounting or Finance

• Minimum 2 years accounting or bookkeeping experience

• Strong understanding of bookkeeping and financial administration

• Excellent organisational and coordination skills

• Strong attention to detail and accountability

• Ability to manage multiple internal operational responsibilities

• Proficiency with accounting software and Microsoft 365 / Google Workspace

• Strong written and verbal communication skills

• Demonstrated ability to take ownership of internal processes and improve operational systems will be highly regarded



Why Join Us

Our group is a growing multi-disciplinary business with strong 
ntum.



This role offers:

• Exposure to internal finance and operational management

• Opportunity to develop into a senior internal management role as the business expands

• Hands-on involvement across multiple businesses and industries

• Long-term career opportunities within an expanding organisation

title: Travel Funnel & Facebook Ads Specialist (Group Travel – High Performance Role)
description:
We are seeking an experienced Funnel Builder +
 Ads Specialist to help us fill a 15–20 traveler luxury South Africa group (June 2026).

This is NOT a basic virtual assistant role.

We are looking for someone who can generate PAID bookings, not just leads.



Project Goal

Fill 15–20 travelers

Timeline: 60 days (30-day accelerated bonus available)

Focus on converting leads into paid clients



Responsibilities

Build a high-converting landing page in Go High Level

Integrate Stripe payment links (service fee required before booking)

Launch and manage
 & Instagram ad campaigns

Generate qualified leads and convert them into paid bookings

Optimize campaigns daily for performance

Track and report daily/weekly results (leads, bookings, cost per conversion)



 Communication & Follow-Up (REQUIRED)

Daily follow-up with all leads is REQUIRED

All new leads must be contacted within a few hours

Follow-ups must continue until conversion or disqualification

Provide daily updates on:

Leads generated

Follow-ups completed

Paid bookings



 Ad Account & Transparency Requirements

You must run ads using your own
 ad account

You must provide access to view campaign performance (Ads Manager or shared reporting)

Full transparency on ad spend and results is REQUIRED

Must be comfortable managing and scaling ad budgets



Compensation

$4–$6/hour (base)

Performance Bonus:

$500 bonus for filling 20 paid bookings within 60 days

$1,000 bonus if 20 bookings are filled within 30 days

Additional bonuses and long-term opportunities available for future group projects.



Requirements (MUST HAVE)

Proven experience running
 Ads with results

Experience building funnels (Go High Level preferred)

Strong understanding of conversion optimization

Fast communication (responses within hours, not once per day)



Experience generating SALES (not just leads)

 To Apply, Answer:

How many PAID bookings have you generated for a project before?

What is your average cost per lead and cost per conversion?

What ad budget would you require to fill 15–20 travelers?

Can you build funnels inside Go High Level?

Can you run ads on your own account and provide access?



Bonus (Preferred)

Experience in travel or luxury travel

Experience selling group travel

Experience with Stripe integrations



We are looking for a long-term partner. If you perform well, you will manage multiple group launches with us.

title: Legal Assistant | Remote | Philippines
description:
Full-Time | Remote | Based in the Philippines

Hours: 9:00 AM – 5:00 P
lbourne time (AEST: 7:00 AM – 3:00 PM PHT)

Monthly Salary: PHP 45,000

???? Apply herehttps://forms.gle/EHwCKoviNikk6dvT8

About the Role

We are hiring a Legal Executive Assistant to support two principal lawyers at a boutique Australian commercial and property law firm. This role combines executive assistant responsibilities with paralegal-level document handling in a premium, relationship-driven practice. You will own client onboarding, document production, search management, file administration, and third-party liaison across both commercial and property law matters. 

This is suited for someone who has worked in an Australian law firm or comparable legal environment, understands legal terminology, and takes genuine pride in accuracy and follow-through. The firm provides a comprehensive workflow manual and structured onboarding — but you must be the kind of person who absorbs training quickly, applies it consistently, and takes ownership of your role.

Key Responsibilities

Client onboarding: opening files, generating cost agreements, conflict checks, electronic signing

Document production from precedent templates in Microsoft Word and practice management software

Property and company searches via InfoTrack — ordering, monitoring, and extracting data

Liaison with councils, water authorities, and third parties by phone and

Invoicing and debtor follow-up from template correspondence

File and matter management: diarising deadlines, tracking progress, proactive follow-ups

Answering client calls via Australian VoIP number — confirming details, taking messages

Occasional slide preparation for speaking engagements and bio updates

What Success Looks Like

Documents go out with correct names, dates, amounts, and execution clauses every time

Tasks are completed end-to-end before moving on — nothing left half-finished

Search results are monitored and flagged proactively — not discovered 10 days late

Follow-ups are diarised and actioned without being asked

Principals trust you enough to delegate without double-checking every output

You bring ideas for improving workflows and processes

What We're Looking For

Experience in an Australian law firm or equivalent legal environment (property or commercial law

preferred)

Working knowledge of legal concepts: vendor, purchaser, settlement, cost agreement, conveyancing,

execution clauses

Expert-level Microsoft Word: formatting, cross-referencing, tables of contents, styles, consistent fonts

Strong attention to detail with a habit of self-checking before submission

Proactive, reliable, and able to work independently once trained

Comfortable on the phone with Australian clients and government authorities

Professional written and spoken English

Available and consistent during Australian business hours

Receptive to feedback and demonstrates sustained improvement over time

Software You'll Use

Action Step (legal practice management)

Microsoft Word, PowerPoint, and Office Suite

InfoTrack (property and company searches)

Asana (task management)

Adobe (electronic document signing)

Why This Role 

Work directly with two experienced principal lawyers who invest in their team

Certified B Corp firm with strong values, generous leave, and genuine flexibility

Comprehensive workflow manual and structured training provided

Philippine-friendly hours — finish by mid-afternoon PHT

Opportunity to develop paralegal-level property law skills

Stable, long-term role with a firm that wants to hire once and keep you

Next Step 

Shortlisted candidates will be asked to complete a practical skills assessment including a Microsoft Word formatting and accuracy test. Please complete the application form in full. Incomplete applications will not be reviewed.

title: Legal Drafter – Family Law Specialist
description:
Legal Drafter – Family Law Specialist

Full-Time | Remote | Based in the Philippines | Australian Eastern Daylight Time

Monthly Salary: PHP 45,000

???? Apply here: 



About the Role

We’re hiring a skilled and experienced Family Law Specialist to support our team through high-volume legal drafting focused on Australian family law. This is a role for someone who thrives on precision, structure, and fast-paced legal work — not a stepping-stone, but a long-term opportunity to deepen your expertise in family law drafting.

You’ll use custom templates and tools to draft agreements and applications across a variety of family law matters, collaborating closely with internal staff to ensure clarity, compliance, and timely delivery. A strong command of Clio is a major advantage, as our team is currently migrating systems and looking for someone who can onboard quickly and confidently.



Key Responsibilities

? Draft applications and agreements related to divorce, financial settlements, parenting plans, and prenuptial arrangements

? Use our custom-built drafting software and templates to generate clear and accurate legal documents

? Coordinate with internal staff to meet Australian Family Law compliance requirements

? Manage a high volume of drafting assignments with accuracy and speed

? Follow internal quality-control processes to minimize errors and protect client outcomes

? Adapt to internal systems and tools — including Clio — to streamline legal operations

? Track deadlines, progress matters, and communicate clearly with the broader team



Qualifications & Skills

? Accredited Family Law Specialist with substantial drafting experience

? Strong understanding of Australian family law, including divorce applications, consent orders, and financial agreements

? Proficiency in Clio or similar legal practice management software is highly preferred

? Exceptional written English and legal drafting skills

? Strong personal systems for managing volume work without sacrificing accuracy

? Responsive, professional, and collaborative team player

? Detail-focused: understands the risk of even small errors in legal drafting

? Comfortable adopting new tools and workflows (training provided)

? Stable remote setup (own computer, reliable internet; second monitor strongly preferred for speed and accuracy)

? Long-term fit: committed to a legal support career track (not pursuing a lawyer pathway)



Work Environment & Schedule

? Remote, full-time role

? Based on Melbourne time

? Communication via Microsoft Teams chat and
, plus occasional calls as required by firm policy

? Embedded in a tight-knit legal team; reports to internal staff based in Australia



Cultural Fit

You thrive in a high-volume, detail-heavy legal environment. You care deeply about accuracy, ownership, and getting things right the first time. You’re motivated by process, structure, and continuous learning — and you value stability, collaboration, and clear expectations.

title: Podcast Editor
description:
I’m looking for a skilled podcast editor with experience in both audio and video editing to support ongoing weekly episodes, which are approximately 50-75 minutes long (raw recording). I use Riverside to record with remote guests or solo episodes.



About the Podcast

This is a thoughtful, conversational podcast featuring coaches, therapists, and business owners sharing both expertise and personal experiences. The tone is:

- natural, conversational 

- reflective

- not overly scripted

- sometimes slower or pauses paced by design (natural conversation flow)

- This is not fast, high-energy content. The goal is depth, clarity, and authenticity.



About the editing: 

- I handle the content edit first (cutting and shaping the conversation).

You will receive a content-edited audio file and will:

- clean and enhance audio quality

- balance levels for both speakers

- remove unnecessary filler (when appropriate)

- preserve natural pauses and conversational flow

- edit video to match the audio seamlessly

- add pre-made intro and outro

- ensure final output is polished but still human

- Create 3 reels that fit with the brand feel, but that will increase engagement (Reel content will be provided)



?? What Matters Most

Audio always comes first (video should never disrupt flow)

- Do NOT over-edit pauses (natural silence is intentional)

- Avoid making speech sound robotic

- Strong judgment > aggressive editing

- This role is about knowing what to leave in, not just what to cut.



? Requirements

- Experience editing podcasts (audio + video)

- Strong understanding of natural conversation pacing

- Attention to detail with sound quality and transitions

- Ability to follow direction and maintain consistency

- Reliable turnaround times



In your application, include:

- A link to your portfolio or samples

- Edit this sample 9-minute video:



- Create a reel with it





???? Additional Notes

This is a long-term role for the right person

Looking for someone who understands subtle, high-quality editing

Communication and consistency matter



If this feels aligned with your style, I’d love to see your work.



Thanks for applying!

About the role



The Blended Group is a talent management and esports company operating multiple brands across entertainment, gaming, and influencer marketing. We are looking for a skilled and self-motivated part-time Virtual Assistant to support our Social Media Manager across all active platforms for 4 hours per day. This role is ideal for someone who blends creativity with data-driven thinking — someone who can write compelling copy, design eye-catching visuals, and report on what is actually working.



Platforms you will manage

Instagram

 (including Meta Ads Manager)

title: Senior Bookkeeper - US Government Contracting / Real Estate / Multi-LLC
description:
Senior Bookkeeper



Part-Time (~20 hrs/wk)  |  Remote — Philippines-Based  |  QuickBooks Desktop + Online Required



About You ---------------------



You don't just keep books — you understand them. You know what a clean set of financials looks like, and more importantly, you know when something's off before anyone else does.



You've handled the financial complexity of U.S.-based businesses before — project invoicing, multi-entity reconciliation, certification support, the works. You take pride in a smooth month-end close and digital binders that are always audit-ready.



You don't wait to be told there's a problem. You spot the discrepancy, you track it down, and you flag it with context and a recommended fix. You're comfortable operating with significant autonomy, and you communicate clearly when you need something.



If you're a meticulous, experienced bookkeeper who wants a long-term engagement with a complex, growing organization — and you're ready to own the books the right way — keep reading.





About Performance Excellence Partners ---------------------



Performance Excellence Partners (PEP) is a multimillion-dollar, woman-owned government contracting agency. We execute complex federal contracts, manage a growing real estate portfolio across multiple LLCs, and operate with the financial discipline that government contracting demands. The books are currently clean and well-structured — your job is to keep them that way and take them further.





About the Role ---------------------



We are seeking a senior bookkeeper based in the Philippines to take full ownership of PEP's day-to-day financial operations, including the government contracting entity and all real estate LLC accounts. This is a part-time engagement (~20 hours/week) with the expectation of dedicated, consistent hours and clear deliverables.



You will work alongside our CPA (who handles oversight, advisory, and strategic financial matters) and coordinate with our HR PEO for payroll cross-checking. You will report directly to Rachel, the CEO.





What You'll Do ---------------------



Daily & Weekly Operations

AP and AR entry into QuickBooks — accurate, timely, well-categorized

Bank deposits recorded accurately

Cross-check our PEO payroll output against TeamKeeper timesheets for accuracy

Review and prepare travel reimbursements and consultant invoices for payment

Review travel reports for accuracy before submission

Flag discrepancies, missing documentation, or unusual items proactively



Invoicing & Contract Billing



Create project invoices aligned to government contract task orders and billing milestones

Cross-check all invoices in QuickBooks (Book to Bill) — ensure billing matches the books

Import and reconcile timesheets from TeamKeeper to contracts

Track budget realignments and support preparation and submission



Monthly Close & Reporting



Reconcile bank accounts and AMEX on a consistent monthly cadence

Prepare month-end balance sheet and PTO schedules

Maintain and update digital financial binders — organized and audit-ready

Support monthly financial reporting package for Rachel's review



Property & Multi-Entity Accounts



Reconcile all PEP property LLC accounts — separate and clean

Track property-level P&L, expenses, and owner distributions

Support migration of property accounts to QuickBooks Online as directed



Compliance & Tax Prep Support



Prepare 1099s annually for eligible vendors and contractors

Gather and organize all tax return documents — deliver to CPA on schedule

Manage corporate certification applications, renewals, and financial documentation

Support RFI, RFP, and personal wealth statement financial data assembly



Strategic Support (Level 2)



Update and develop accounting processes and procedures documentation

Support development of government proposal budgets alongside the CPA

Assist with annual forecast, pricing proposals, and provisional billing rate preparation





A Day in the Life ---------------------



You log in and run through the prior day's transactions — AP entries are confirmed, a bank deposit needs to be recorded, and there's a consultant invoice from last week that doesn't match the QuickBooks record. You pull the original contract, identify the discrepancy, and send Rachel a brief Loom explaining what you found and how you'd like to resolve it.



You import this week's TeamKeeper timesheets, allocate hours to the appropriate contracts, and flag that one employee logged 6 hours to a contract that's in closeout — you note it for Rachel to confirm before payroll cross-check.



You close the afternoon with monthly reconciliation on two property LLCs and update the certification tracker — one renewal is coming up in 45 days, so you start gathering the financial documents needed for the application.





What We're Looking For ---------------------



Minimum 6 years of bookkeeping experience — U.S.-based businesses strongly preferred

Direct experience with government contracting, job costing, or project-based billing strongly preferred

QuickBooks Desktop proficiency required; QuickBooks Online a strong plus; migration experience ideal

Experience managing books for multiple LLCs or separate entities simultaneously

Real estate bookkeeping experience is a significant advantage

Familiarity with TeamKeeper, ADP, or similar timesheet/payroll systems helpful

Bachelor's degree in accounting, finance, or related field preferred

CPA or equivalent credential is a strong differentiator

Excellent written English — clear, professional, and concise





Schedule & Compensation ---------------------



Part-time, approximately 20 hours per week with consistent, dedicated availability

Flexible scheduling with 2–3 hours daily overlap with U.S. Pacific Time

Compensation: $10–$15 USD/hour based on experience (higher end leaning toward CPA skills)

Paid monthly via direct bank transfer

Long-term engagement — potential to grow to full-time as PEP scales



"We're not looking for someone who can 'handle it.' We're looking for someone who will own it."





How To Apply  ---------------------



Please proceed to our application via the Jotform Link below. 



Apply Here: 



Thank you in advance for taking the time to help us get to know you.

title: Experienced Graphic Designer / Photo Retoucher
description:
What is it like to work with us? HEAR FROM OUR TEAM! 

Why should you partner with us? WE ARE NOT YOUR ORDINARY CLIENT! (aka #NotYourOrdinaryAFAM lol) 



We’re hiring an experienced Graphic Designer and Photo Retoucher with real estate experience who understands how to create clean, professional, high-converting visuals across digital and print.



This role is for someone who knows how to design with clarity and purpose. You understand branding, layout, hierarchy, and how visuals influence attention and trust, especially in real estate marketing.

READ THE ENTIRE JOB LISTING CAREFULLY. The final step of the hiring process will require the candidate to provide at least 1 former employer/team lead reference and at least 1 character reference. These will be verified prior to extending an offer.



--- ABOUT THE TEAM ---

Want to work in a rewarding, fast-paced, start-up atmosphere? Looking for significant growth and performance bonus opportunities? Have a growth mindset, value relationships, and dedicated to getting results? Then we might be a fit!



What is it like to work with us? HEAR FROM OUR TEAM!   





Our Team Core Values:

1) DELIGHT THE CLIENT:  We consistently exceed expectations by anticipating needs, delivering outstanding service, and creating those “magic 
nts” where every client feels seen and heard.

2) MASTER YOUR CRAFT:  We commit to continuous learning, share our expertise openly, and tackle every challenge with precision and a deep sense of duty.

3) FOLLOW THROUGH, ALWAYS:  When we say something will happen, it does—every time. We own every task, ensuring that nothing falls through the cracks, and when mistakes occur, we learn and improve immediately.



Our core values are important, and the right candidate will share our values. We are building an awesome place to work, not just managing marketing campaigns for our real estate business.



--- ABOUT YOU ---

Do you have real estate design experience and understand what makes listings visually credible and market-ready? Do you know how to design social posts and brochures that look clean, professional, and on-brand without overcomplicating them? Do you move fast when timelines are tight and still pay attention to spacing, alignment, and small details?



Then keep reading to see if we're a fit!



Responsibilities:

Social & Digital Design

- Design YouTube branding assets including thumbnails, banners, and episode graphics.

- Create Instagram listing posts, announcements, ads, and story graphics.

- Develop and maintain branded social templates for consistent output.



Print Design

- Design real estate yard signs and signage.

- Create listing brochures and marketing flyers.

- Prepare clean, print-ready files with correct sizing and bleed settings.



Real Estate Photo Editing

- Edit listing photos for lighting, color correction, and clarity.

- Clean up images by removing distractions and enhancing presentation.

- Ensure final images look professional while remaining realistic.



Brand Consistency & Organization

- Maintain consistent fonts, colors, and layout standards

- Ensure all visuals align with company branding

- Organize design files clearly for future use



Requirements:

- Proven experience in real estate graphic design, required

- Strong portfolio showing real estate social and print work, required

- Experience with Adobe Creative Suite (Photoshop, Illustrator, Lightroom), or similar tools

- Strong understanding of layout, spacing, and visual hierarchy

- Experience preparing print-ready files

- Experience editing real estate photos

- Ability to work quickly without sacrificing quality

- Strong communication skills

- Ability to follow brand guidelines

- A stable, high-speed internet connection

- Growth mindset



BONUS SKILLS (NOT REQUIRED)

- Light motion graphics

- Understanding of social media performance metrics

- Basic copy awareness (headline spacing, readability, CTA placement)



NOTE: This is a project-based role with the opportunity to grow into a full-time position for the right person.



--- APPLICATION & RECRUITMENT PROCESS ---

STEP 1:

Fill out the application form through the link below and write "green kangaroo" in the Additional Comments field.







STEP 2:

Qualified candidates may be invited to participate in two PAID test projects, $10-20 USD for each test project estimated to be 2-3hrs of work for each (a total of $20-40 USD!).



STEP 3:

Candidates who advance through the test project rounds will be invited to an interview.



STEP 4:

Before extending a trial offer, we will conduct a CHARACTER REFERENCE CHECK for the final candidate(s). CONDUCTING A REFERENCE CHECK will be the last step in the hiring process.



STEP 5:

Upon receiving a trial offer, you will be onboarded as a Graphic Designer with a 90-day PAID trial period. During this period, we expect you to demonstrate your editing expertise, meet project deadlines, be a team player, and excel at what you do. A successful trial will lead to a permanent offer to join the team.

title: Level 2 - IT Support Specialist & Client Support
description:
Job Title: Level 2 – IT Support Specialist & Client Support 

Work Setup: Fully Remote 

Work Schedule: Monday – Friday, Pacific Standard Time 

Salary: $1,800 - $2,000 



Role Overview 

This is a Help Desk and Technical Support role with a strong emphasis on client communication. The person in this role handles the majority of day-to-day support interactions with startup teams; they’re the voice clients hear most often. Technical chops matter, but so does the ability to make a stressed-out employee feel taken care of. 

If you are the type who can resolve a Google Workspace sync issue, walk a remote employee through a fix over Zoom, and close the ticket with documentation that a colleague could follow, all while keeping the client feeling supported, not talked down to - YOU belong here. 



This Role IS: 

The face of our company to technologically sophisticated, security-conscious clients — you own relationships, not just tickets 

Equal parts technical problem-solving and warm, clear client communication 

A Mac-first, Google Workspace-first, SaaS-security environment 

Autonomy with accountability — startup clients expect fast, independent judgment 



This Role IS NOT: 

A backend NOC or infrastructure role where you work silently behind the scenes 

A pure engineering role — if you prefer zero client contact, this is not the right fit 

A Windows Server / Active Directory / on-prem data center environment 

A role with heavy supervision and rigid escalation paths 



Responsibilities: 

Technical Delivery 

Provide?Tier 2 support?for escalated issues across Mac workstations, networks, Google Workspace, and SaaS platforms 

Troubleshoot and resolve i
 independently with clear, client-readable documentation 

Collaborate with Tier 1 and Tier 3 teams to resolve issues and share knowledge 

Support?client onboarding workflows: device enrollment, MDM configuration, account provisioning, and Laptop Logistics coordination 

Support monitoring, patching, and alert response workflows — including user-facing CVE remediation notifications 

Participate in backup testing and maintenance routines 

Contribute to SOP and knowledge base documentation improvements 



Client Experience 

Serve as the?front line of client interaction?— every support touchpoint shapes how clients feel about Ignition 

Communicate clearly and warmly with non-technical end-users (startup employees, ops teams, office managers) via chat,
, video, and phone 

Set expectations proactively — no silent gaps; if a fix takes time, the client knows what's happening and when to expect an update 

Own client communication through escalations — clients should never feel passed off without context 

Write ticket notes and follow-ups that a non-technical reader can easily understand 



Required Qualifications 

Technical Qualifications 

6+ years of IT support experience, with substantial time at an IT Managed Service Provider (MSP) 

Strong?Mac/macOS support?experience?(candidates without extensive Apple experience must obtain Apple Desktop Support certification within 90 days) 

Experience administering?Google Workspace or Microsoft 365?for remote/hybrid workforces 

Experience with?endpoint management/MDM tools?(Jamf, Intune, or similar) 

Strong?cybersecurity literacy: familiarity with zero-trust SaaS security platforms such as SentinelOne, Okta, Entra ID, Cisco Umbrella, KnowBe4, or equivalent 

Solid?networking fundamentals?(VPNs, firewalls, routing), ideally with Cisco Meraki experience 

Strong troubleshooting methodology with consistent documentation habits 



Client-Facing Skills?(Equally Weighted) 

Demonstrated ability to explain technical issues to non-technical people in plain, reassuring language 

Experience in a client-facing support role where customer satisfaction was measured — and you scored well (NPS, CSAT, or equivalent) 

Patience and empathy when supporting end-users who are frustrated, stressed, or unfamiliar with technology — these are non-negotiable 

Clear, professional written communication across tickets,
s, and follow-ups 



Soft Skills 

Strong time management and ability to manage multiple tickets across multiple client accounts simultaneously 

Team-first mindset — you share knowledge, flag patterns, and actively help teammates improve 

Accountability and attention to detail — if you commit to a follow-up, you follow through 

Proactive communicator — you never wait for a client to chase you 



Benefits:

a. 13th Month pay

b. Health Stipend

c. Government Contribution Allowance

d. Personal Time Off (PTO)

title: Creative Strategist | Direct Response Copywriter
description:
About Us:

We are a dynamic and rapidly growing 7-figure women's jewelry brand powered by Shopify. We are passionate about great design that speaks for itself, drawing inspiration from everyday fashion and influencers, and genuinely caring about the products we create and the relationships we build. We operate in a fast-paced environment where data drives decisions, and creative excellence fuels our growth.



The Role:

Forget random ideas that look pretty but don't sell. We're looking for a sharp, data-driven Creative Strategist/Direct Response Copywriter (full time) who understands that killer creative is the engine of profitable scaling. You will be responsible for developing, testing, and iterating on ad creatives (video & static) across platforms like Meta and TikTok, working closely with media buyers, video editors, graphic designers and creators to maximize conversions and crush performance targets. You live and breathe direct response principles and know how to turn one winning concept into twenty variations that print money.



Key Responsibilities:



• Data-Driven Creative Analysis: Dive deep into ad account metrics (Meta, TikTok, etc.) to understand what's working, what's not, and why. Spot trends, form hypotheses, and communicate insights clearly to media buyers.



• High-Impact Copywriting: Craft compelling hooks, angles, and calls-to-action that drive engagement and conversions. Master prompting AI tools for efficiency, but possess the skill to refine copy until it's razor-sharp.



• Iterative Concept Development: Take winning creative concepts and strategically develop numerous variations (different hooks, visuals, CTAs, angles) for rigorous testing. Every test must have a clear goal.



• Creative Production Oversight: Guide video editors/graphic designers, videographers, and UGC creators to ensure final ad assets are not just aesthetically pleasing, but optimized for platform best practices and performance goals. Understand the difference between raw production and effective post-production.



• Trend Spotting & Application: Keep your finger on the pulse of e-commerce advertising trends, competitor strategies, and platform changes, translating insights into actionable creative tests.



• Collaborative Execution: Work seamlessly with media buyers, designers, editors, and potentially the operations team to ensure a smooth and efficient creative workflow from concept to launch.



Who We're Looking For:



• Data Mastery is Non-Negotiable: Proven experience analyzing ad performance data (ROAS, CPA, CTR, CVR, etc.) within ad platforms (Meta Ads Manager is essential). You must be comfortable talking numbers and deriving actionable insights.



• Direct Response Copywriting Chops: Ability to write clear, concise, high-impact ad copy that drives action. Experience refining AI-generated copy is a plus, but strong fundamental writing skill is key.



• Creativity That Converts: You don't just generate ideas; you understand how to systematically test and iterate on creative concepts to find winners and scale them effectively.



• Production & Post-Production Awareness: You understand the creative workflow and can provide clear direction to editors/creators to ensure the final output aligns with strategic goals and performance needs.



• Execution Mindset: Background demonstrating speed, adaptability, and the ability to perform under pressure (e.g., agency experience, high-growth DTC, performance marketing roles). Experience in fields requiring quick thinking (writing, comedy, even team sports) is valued.



• Big-Picture Thinker, Agile Executor: You see market trends but can rapidly pivot and adapt creative strategy based on real-time data and changing conditions.



• Accountable & Proactive: You take ownership of your work, meet deadlines consistently, and proactively identify opportunities or solve problems without needing hand-holding.



• Clear, Efficient Communicator: You can articulate creative strategy and feedback clearly and concisely via Slack,
, or calls.



• Highly Collaborative: You thrive working within a team environment.



• E-commerce Obsessed: You actively follow top e-commerce brands, performance marketers, ad breakdowns, and UGC trends to constantly sharpen your skills.



Compensation:

$3,000/Month plus performance-based bonus/incentives tied directly to creative contribution towards advertising profitability.



How to Apply:



In your proposal, please include:



1. Provide your Resume/CV and a link to your portfolio (if applicable) or examples of previous ad creative work/case studies.



2. Analyze This (Hypothetical): An ad creative has a high CTR (5%) but a low CVR (0.5%) leading to a poor ROAS. Briefly outline 2-3 potential reasons for this based on creative/copy and suggest specific improvements you'd test.



3. Write This: Provide one strong hook and one clear call-to-action for a video ad promoting a new collection of affordable luxury necklaces for a woman aged 25-45 that has a busy lifestyle so she wants easy styling. She's been burned by cheap gold jewelry before. 



4. How Do You Learn?: Briefly describe how you stay current with DTC/e-commerce advertising trends and creative best practices (e.g., who do you follow, what resources do you use?).



5. What is your desired monthly salary in USD (provide number or won't be considered for the job)?

title: Meta Ads Director
description:
META ADS DIRECTOR (AI-FIRST / ANDROMEDA-ERA)



THE ROLE

- We are hiring a Meta Ads Director to take full ownership of paid social strategy and execution across our client portfolio.

- This is not a traditional media buyer role and it is not a button-pusher role.

- You must understand how Meta Ads work in 2026. That means you understand the shift to an AI-first ad delivery environment where campaign success is driven less by manual audience micromanagement and more by creative strength, creative diversification, signal quality, incrementality, and strategic optimization inside Meta’s evolving ad system.



You should be fluent in how Meta’s current advertising environment has changed campaign planning, testing, and scaling. You should understand the implications of Andromeda, Advantage+ automation, faster AI-driven optimization cycles, stronger ranking systems, and the growing importance of creative as a core targeting and performance lever.



We are looking for someone who knows that:

- Creative is key

- Creative diversification is no longer optional



Winning on Meta in 2026 requires strong strategy, clean signals, and a creative testing system

CPL alone is not success

CAC, qualified pipeline, booked calls, sales, and ROI are what matter



You will work directly with the Founder and alongside a lean, high-performance team that values speed, precision, initiative, and results. We need someone who sees problems early, spots optimization opportunities quickly, and acts before performance declines.



WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE IN THIS ROLE



The right person for this role:



Understands how to build and scale Meta campaigns in today’s AI-driven environment

Knows how to structure campaigns so Meta’s system has the right inputs to optimize effectively

Treats creative strategy as a primary growth lever, not an afterthought

Can evaluate, analyze, and optimize creative performance across every stage of the funnel

Knows the difference between a weak creative, a fatigued creative, a misaligned offer, and a signal/tracking problem

Makes decisions based on business outcomes, not vanity metrics

Can connect ad performance to lead quality, sales quality, CAC, and ROI

Operates with urgency, ownership, and independent thinking

CORE RESPONSIBILITIES

Strategy & Campaign Architecture

Own the end-to-end Meta Ads strategy for every client account, from audit and research to campaign architecture, scaling, and performance management.

Build campaign structures that reflect how Meta Ads work in 2026, using automation, broad inputs, strong conversion signals, and creative-led strategy instead of outdated over-segmentation.

Develop full-funnel paid social strategies across prospecting, retargeting, and re-engagement.

Map campaigns to buyer awareness stages and ensure the right message, angle, format, and offer are shown at the right stage of the journey.

Build testing roadmaps for offers, hooks, messaging angles, formats, landing pages, and conversion objectives.

Creative Strategy, Evaluation & Diversification

Lead the creative strategy behind every campaign.

Build and maintain a creative diversification system that includes multiple concepts, hooks, personas, proof mechanisms, formats, visuals, and CTAs.

Continuously evaluate, analyze, and optimize creatives based on performance data, funnel stage, audience response, and conversion quality.

Identify why a creative is underperforming and determine whether the issue is the hook, angle, offer, format, message match, fatigue, or conversion friction.

Work closely with designers, editors, and copywriters to produce creatives that are strategically different, not just visually different.

Ensure a regular cadence of creative testing and refreshes to avoid fatigue and keep campaigns competitive.

Write and direct high-performing ad copy that aligns with the audience’s psychology, awareness stage, and intent level.

Campaign Management & Optimization

Manage day-to-day Meta Ads operations across multiple client accounts.

Monitor campaigns daily and act quickly on leading indicators such as rising CPMs, declining CTR, falling conversion rates, weak lead quality, or frequency issues.

Make strategic optimizations across budget allocation, campaign structure, creative rotation, exclusions, retargeting logic, and offer positioning.

Know when to simplify, consolidate, scale, or cut.

Use performance data to guide creative decisions, not just media-buying decisions.

Tracking, Attribution & Signal Quality

Maintain strong campaign hygiene across naming conventions, UTMs, event prioritization, Pixel, Conversions API, CRM attribution, and reporting systems.

Ensure Meta has the strongest possible data inputs to optimize against.

Diagnose tracking gaps, poor attribution, or weak event quality before they distort decision-making.

Understand how to measure true performance beyond platform-reported numbers.

Reporting & Business Impact

Report on the metrics that actually matter: CAC, qualified lead rate, booked appointment rate, show-up rate, close rate, revenue attribution, and ROI.

Build reports that explain what happened, why it happened, and what actions are being taken next.

Present strategic recommendations clearly and confidently to leadership and, when needed, directly to clients.

Stay focused on profitable scaling, not cosmetic reporting.

AI & Workflow Leverage

Use AI tools as force multipliers for ideation, analysis, copy, research, reporting, and operational efficiency.

Stay current with Meta’s evolving ad environment and AI-driven changes to campaign execution and creative performance.

Use AI to improve speed and quality, while still applying strong strategic judgment.

REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS & EXPERIENCE

Minimum 4+ years of hands-on Meta Ads experience with a proven record of generating leads and revenue for service-based businesses.

Strong understanding of how to succeed running Meta Ads in 2026, including AI-first delivery, creative-led performance, automation, signal quality, and modern attribution.

Clear understanding of Andromeda-era Meta Ads strategy and how current changes affect campaign structure, audience strategy, testing, optimization, and scaling.

Strong belief that creative is key, with the ability to build systems for creative diversification across concepts, formats, messaging angles, and awareness stages.

Strong ability to evaluate, analyze, and optimize creatives for each campaign to improve performance.

Strong copywriting and messaging skills with a track record of writing ad copy that drives real business results.

Deep experience with Meta Ads Manager, Business Manager, Events Manager, Pixel, CAPI, Custom Audiences, Lookalikes, and attribution workflows.

Strong analytical ability across Meta reporting, landing page performance, CRM outcomes, and downstream sales data.

Experience managing multiple accounts at once in an agency environment without losing strategic depth or attention to detail.

Excellent English communication skills, both written and verbal.

Strong ownership mentality, proactive communication, and ability to execute without constant oversight.

PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

Experience with Google Ads, TikTok Ads, or YouTube Ads

Familiarity with GoHighLevel, HubSpot, Salesforce, or similar CRMs

Experience with lead nurture systems, SMS/email follow-up, and funnel optimization

Experience with landing page testing and CRO

Meta Blueprint Certification

Experience building reporting dashboards in Looker Studio, DashThis, Hyros, Triple Whale, or similar tools

THE MINDSET WE’RE LOOKING FOR



We are not looking for someone who hides behind dashboards, blames the algorithm, or waits for instructions.



We are looking for someone who:



takes ownership

thinks strategically

moves fast

catches issues early

understands that great Meta performance is now driven by creative quality, creative variation, strong signals, and smart optimization

cares about profitability and client results, not vanity metrics



You should be comfortable in a fast-moving agency environment where expectations are high and performance matters.



HOW TO APPLY



To apply, please submit:



Your updated resume/CV

A portfolio or case study deck showing 2–3 Meta lead generation campaigns you managed for service-based businesses

A 3–5 minute Loom video introducing yourself and walking through one campaign you are proud of

Examples of ad copy you have written

A short summary of how you use AI in your workflow

In your Loom video, you must also answer:

How has Meta’s newest ad delivery environment changed the way you build campaigns?

What does creative diversification mean to you in practice?

How do you d
 whether a creative problem is caused by the hook, the offer, fatigue, poor signal quality, or bad funnel alignment?

Why is CAC and ROI more important than CPL when evaluating Meta campaign performance?



Applications without a Loom video, case studies, and direct answers to the questions above will not be considered.

title: Do you love making people feel cared for? Support our CEOs as an Executive Personal Assistant!
description:
Title: Executive Personal Assistant



Important: Make sure to read the full listing for application instructions.



Do you love making people feel cared for? Are you the one who remembers everyone’s birthdays, plans amazing gatherings, makes fun plans, and brings warmth into the lives of those around you?



This is NOT your typical assistant job. It’s deeply personal. You’ll work with a kind but intense U.S.-based pair of brothers (proven entrepreneurs working as Co-CEOs of the same business) to support their lives, not the company they are building. From keeping friendships alive and planning meaningful experiences to sending thoughtful gifts and helping them stay on track with personal goals, you’ll be part organizer, part cheerleader, part coach. If you’ve ever been called the “mom” of your group and you’re looking for a decades-long position, this might be your dream job.



Your Role

* Build trust by executing on tasks and communicating clearly, which will lead to taking on more responsibility, which will then lead to increases in pay.

* Keep relationships thriving by managing a personal CRM and helping stay in touch with friends and family.

* Use technology to help automate certain tasks that are repetitive.

* Track habits, support routines, and act as an accountability partner for personal goals.

* Pick out thoughtful, creative gifts for birthdays and other special 
nts.

* Research products, tools, and ideas to make life easier, better, or more fun.

* Plan and book unforgettable travel, great hotels, and the perfect dinner spots.

* Jump in on special personal projects wherever a helping hand is needed.



What’s In It for You

* A Long-Term Role: This is meant to last. The entrepreneurs who you'd be an assistant to are looking for a lifetime assistant.

* Great Place to Work: These entrepreneurs are known to have a super high retention rate of employees and create a great work environment.

* Creative Work: From planning memorable trips to picking out the perfect gift, this role lets you flex your creative muscles and thoughtful side every day.

* Variety: No two days will feel the same. You’ll get to research, plan, coach, organize, and more.

* Paid Leave: You'll get at least 10 days of paid leave plus major holidays.

* Remote Flexibility: Work from the comfort of your home and gain flexibility with hours once trust is built.



Desired Attributes

* Extremely caring, social people-person who will excel in personal tasks

* High attention to detail and organizational skills

* Very strong written and oral English skills

* Minimum 50 Mbps Internet Speed

* Designated office space in your home



To apply and ensure your application is considered, do all of the following two steps:

1. DM with a quick introduction to yourself if you're interested. Include the word "cupcake" *in your subject line*, and Include your resume. ++Bonus points for a video introduction, though it's not strictly required

2. Fill out this form:

3.  Add the Letters "PA" in the "What's your full name" field [E.g. PA - John Doe], so we know what role you are applying for.

title: HTML5 Game Developer
description:
About the Project



RAVIA is a location-based AR adventure game where kids earn tokens through outdoor exploration and at-home puzzles. Our app is built on a hybrid architecture — Unity powers the AR experience and Flutter drives the UI.



As part of our core gameplay loop, players encounter Challenge Tokens that trigger interactive puzzle opportunities. We need a talented web game developer to build these puzzles as lightweight HTML5 web apps, loaded directly into the game via a WebView.



What We’re Looking For



Game Development Experience — We want someone who has actually built web games. If you’re a standard web developer without a game development background, this role is not for you.



A Proven Track Record — A portfolio of playable web-based mini-games, puzzles, or interactive experiences is required. Links to live, playable work are essential.



Performance Focus — Your games must load instantly and run at a smooth frame rate on mobile devices without bloating the host app’s memory footprint.



Independence — This is a gig-based role. We need someone who can receive a puzzle concept, build it cleanly, test it, and deliver it integration-ready.





The Gig



You will be responsible for developing a suite of standalone, web-based mini-games spanning Math, Word, and Logic puzzles. Each puzzle must:

- Be fully responsive and optimized to run flawlessly inside a mobile WebView (both iOS and Android).

- Support dynamic configuration for different difficulty levels (Easy, Medium, Hard) and age brackets (6–9, 10–14, 15–17).

- Include a seamless communication bridge to pass completion data — time taken, correct answers, score — back to the host application (Unity / Flutter) for backend token processing.





Technical Requirements



Core Technologies

- Expert proficiency in HTML5, CSS3, and vanilla JavaScript.

- Deep familiarity with the HTML5 Canvas API and/or WebGL for real-time rendering.

- Web Game Frameworks

- Strong hands-on experience with at least one HTML5 game engine or lightweight interactive framework — Phaser, PixiJS, Cocos Creator, or equivalent.

- WebView Integration

- Proven track record of building web apps specifically designed to be embedded inside native mobile apps (iOS / Android) or Unity via WebView packages.

- Understanding of the performance constraints and rendering quirks unique to mobile WebViews.

- Host Communication

- Experience setting up secure cross-window messaging (postMessage / JavascriptInterface / WKScriptMessageHandler) to communicate between the web layer and a native or Unity host container.





Nice to Have

- Experience with adaptive difficulty algorithms.

- Familiarity with Unity’s UniWebView or Flutter’s flutter_inappwebview packages.

- Background in children’s educational software or game UX for younger audiences.





Compensation & Contract Terms



Duration: 2-month contract (8 weeks)

Commitment: 20–40 hours per week

Rate: AUD $8–$14 per hour, depending on experience and portfolio strength

Total estimated contract value: AUD $1,280–$4,480

Scope: 6 puzzle types to be delivered across the contract period



Hours and milestones will be agreed upon before work begins. We will scope each puzzle type together at the start of the engagement. All source code produced is the sole property of the RAVIA team upon delivery.



How to Apply



Please send us the following:

- A brief introduction covering your experience specifically with HTML5 game development and WebView integrations.

- Links to playable examples of your past work. We want to see real games you have shipped — not mockups or screenshots.



We will review all portfolios and reach out to promising candidates to scope and discuss the first batch of puzzle prototypes.
