title: Experienced Google Ads & Meta Ads Account Manager (Multiple Accounts))
description:
Looking for your next opportunity to do meaningful, high-impact paid advertising work? We're a growing U.S.-based digital marketing agency managing paid advertising across Google and Meta for a diverse roster of clients - e-commerce brands, local service businesses, law firms, real estate, nonprofits, and more. We need a skilled ads manager who can jump in and start managing client ad accounts.



What You'll Be Doing:



You'll be managing multiple client ad accounts across Google Ads and/or Meta Ads. Day-to-day work includes campaign builds, optimization, budget management, audience targeting, ad copy/creative collaboration, and performance reporting. The focus is always on driving real business results - leads, sales, ROAS - not vanity metrics.

You'll get to work across a range of industries, so the work stays fresh and your experience keeps growing.



Who We're Looking For:



We're looking for someone who already knows the platforms well and is excited to put their skills to work. You should be comfortable independently managing campaigns, spotting opportunities, diagnosing issues, and making smart optimization decisions. Specifically, we'd love to see hands-on experience with campaign setup and structure across Search, Shopping, Performance Max, and/or Meta (Advantage+, retargeting, full-funnel setups). A solid understanding of conversion tracking (Google Tag Manager, Meta Pixel/CAPI) is important, along with a results-first mindset (you optimize toward cost per lead, cost per acquisition, and ROAS), not just impressions and clicks. Experience managing multiple accounts in an agency or freelance setting and strong communication skills round things out.



Bonus Points For:



Experience with Google Merchant Center, Shopping feeds, CallRail or call tracking platforms, landing page feedback/CRO thinking, or Klaviyo/email platform familiarity.



Why Join Us:



Your work will directly impact the success of the businesses we serve, and that's something we take a lot of pride in. We believe in clear communication, mutual respect, and giving our team the autonomy to do their best work. We value people who take ownership, and we reward that with trust and room to grow.

As we continue to expand, there's real opportunity here to take on larger accounts, develop your skills across new verticals, and step into a senior or leadership role within the team.



To Apply:



I'd love to hear from you. Please tell me about your experience managing Google and/or Meta ad accounts. Include specific examples of results you've driven (leads generated, ROAS achieved, cost per acquisition improvements, etc.). If you have any case studies or screenshots, even better.

Thank you!

title: Virtual Assistant (Medical Prior Authorizations – Full-Time, Remote)
description:
Monarch Health & Wellness is seeking a reliable, detail-oriented Virtual Assistant to join our growing medical clinic team. This role will temporarily support our current VA during maternity leave, with the opportunity to transition into a long-term position within our company.



We are looking for someone who already understands medication prior authorizations—this is not an entry-level admin role.



POSITION OVERVIEW:

This position is primarily responsible for managing medication prior authorizations, along with supporting daily administrative and clinical workflow tasks.



You will be trained using Loom videos and detailed SOPs, but prior hands-on experience with prior authorizations is required.



KEY RESPONSIBLITIES:

Submit and manage prior authorizations using Cover
ds and other payer portals- daily

Complete PA requests via phone, insurance websites, and PBM portals

Handle medication prior auths including GLP-1s such as Wegovy and Zepbound

Track approvals, denials, and follow-ups

Upload and communicate PA outcomes to providers via EHR

Verify insurance benefits 

Perform data entry (labs, patient documentation, chart updates)

Pull weekly and monthly reports

Assist with additional administrative tasks as needed



REQUIREMENTS (NON-NEGOTIABLE):

Experience completing medication prior authorizations using Cover
ds and similar platforms

Strong attention to detail and accuracy

Comfortable navigating multiple insurance portals and systems

Ability to follow SOPs and work independently

Strong communication skills (written and verbal)

Strong data entry skills



PREFERRED EXPERIENCE:

Prior work in a medical clinic, med spa, or telehealth environment

Experience with insurance verification and benefits checks

Familiarity with GLP-1 medications and weight loss treatments

Familiarity with Square POS, Charm EHR, Spruce phone systems



SCHEDULE:

Full-time (remote)- Starting ASAP

Hours: 7:30 AM – 5:30/6:00 PM CST Mon-Thursday, 7:30am-2:00pm Friday (during business hours)

Temporary requirement: Must be available 7 days/week

Weekends require only a few hours (1-3 hours on Saturday/Sundays) for prior authorizations



COMPENSATION: 

$5.00/hour during training

$6.00/hour after training completion

Opportunity for long-term growth and expanded responsibilities



TRAINING:

Structured onboarding with:

Loom video training

Step-by-step SOPs

Support from current Virtual Assistants located in the Philippines



WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR:

We want someone dependable who takes ownership of their work. This role is critical to patient care and clinic operations—if prior authorizations fall behind, everything backs up.



If you already know how to get approvals done efficiently and can stay organized without constant supervision, you’ll do well here.



TO APPLY, PLEASE INCLUDE:

Your experience with prior authorizations (specific platforms used)

Examples of medications you’ve worked on

Your availability (including weekends)

title: Essay Editor/Academic Writer
description:
We’re an academic consulting firm that helps high school students gain admission to top-tier universities in the U.S., and we’re currently seeking a part-time Essay Editor/Academic Writer with strong writing, editing, and critical thinking skills to join our team.



This role is ideal for someone who can take rough or underdeveloped student essays and turn them into polished, well-structured pieces that read naturally and professionally. The position is part-time and flexible, but it’s also deadline-driven and requires strong reliability, responsiveness, and attention to detail.



This is not a general content writing role. We’re specifically looking for someone who can revise and rewrite essays so they are clearer, stronger, and more compelling while still aligning with the original prompt, intended message, and required word count.



Key Responsibilities:

• Edit and rewrite high school-level essays based on specific prompts and word count requirements, improving structure, clarity, grammar, tone, and overall impact.

• Manage multiple essay assignments at once while meeting tight turnaround times, typically within 24–48 hours, and communicate proactively regarding deadlines or issues that may affect delivery.



Applicants should be able to:

• Rewrite and strengthen weak or underdeveloped drafts without losing the original intent, while elevating flow, structure, readability, and overall quality.

• Demonstrate excellent grammar, sentence control, and word choice, and write with a level of polish that reads as fluent and natural in English.



To proceed with your application, please send a message with your resume, 2-3 writing samples for review, and an estimate of how many hours you can commit each week.

title: Amazon FBA Operations & Lead Analysis Specialist (C1/C2 English)
description:
Are you a detail-oriented Amazon FBA expert who loves solving puzzles and finding profitable opportunities? We are a growing Amazon business looking for a dedicated Operations & Lead Analysis VA to become the backbone of our daily workflow.



This is not a "simple" data entry role. We need someone who understands the nuances of Account Health, knows how to fight for reimbursements, and can analyze Keepa charts like a pro.



Key Responsibilities

1. Account Health & Operations



Case Management: Open and relentlessly follow up on cases with Seller Support regarding listing errors, Brand Registry, and shipping discrepancies.



Account Health Monitoring: Daily checks of the dashboard to catch policy violations or IP complaints before they become problems.



Reimbursements: Audit lost/damaged inventory and file claims to recover funds.



2. Inventory & Supply Chain



Stock Tracking: Monitor levels to prevent OOS (Out of Stock) scenarios or excessive long-term storage fees.



Supplier Relations: Coordinate with retailers and prep centers regarding missing shipments or damaged goods.



3. Lead Approval & Analysis (Critical)

You will act as the "Gatekeeper" for our sourcing team. You will review leads from our Sourcing VA and approve them based on our strict Buy Filters



Candidate Requirements

English Fluency: C1 or C2 level (Written and Verbal). You must be able to communicate clearly with Amazon Support and US-based retailers.



Experience: At least 1-2 years of experience in Amazon OA (Online Arbitrage) or Wholesale.



Technical Skills: Expert-level knowledge of Keepa, Seller Central, and Google Sheets.



Mindset: Analytical, proactive, and detail-oriented.





We value attention to detail above all else. To demonstrate that you have read this entire listing and can follow instructions perfectly:



Subject Line: Your application must start with the word cheesecake followed by your full name.



Introduction: Tell us about a difficult time in your role as an Amazon VA and how you overcame it.



Analysis Test: Briefly explain why we avoid listings where Amazon is a constant seller, even if the ROI looks good. Are there any exceptions where you would still recommend the buy?



Proof: Attach or link your English proficiency results and a brief video introduction of yourself.



Applications that do not follow the subject line instruction will be automatically disqualified.



Performance-Based Scalability: We are a fast-growing company. As you hit your KPIs (Key Performance Indicators) and help the business scale, your compensation will be reviewed to reflect your impact.



We look forward to meeting our next tea
mber!

title: Direct Response Video Editor For Meta/TikTok Ads
description:
About Us:

We are a dynamic and rapidly growing 7-figure women's jewelry brand powered by Shopify. We are passionate about great design that speaks for itself, drawing inspiration from everyday fashion and influencers, and genuinely caring about the products we create and the relationships we build. Our marketing is performance-driven, and high-converting video creative is central to our scaling strategy.



The Role:

We're seeking a skilled and agile Direct Response Video Editor (full time / 40 hours per week) who understands how to create video ads that don't just look good but convert. You will work in lockstep with our Creative Strategist, taking raw footage (UGC, potentially studio shots) and briefs to produce engaging, platform-optimized video ads primarily for Meta 
/Instagram) and TikTok. Your job is to execute rapidly, iterate effectively based on performance insights (provided by the strategist), and ensure the final output is engineered for maximum engagement and conversions. Forget long-form content; this is about short, punchy, direct-response video.



Key Responsibilities:



• Edit High-Performing Video Ads: Transform raw footage and strategist briefs into compelling video ads optimized for Meta and TikTok feeds/stories/reels.



• Rapid Iteration: Quickly execute multiple variations of winning ad concepts provided by the Creative Strategist (testing different hooks, visuals, text overlays, CTAs, music). Understand that iteration based on data is key.



• Platform Optimization: Expertly edit for platform best practices – strong hooks within the first 3 seconds, fast pacing, engaging captions/text overlays, appropriate aspect ratios (9:16, 1:1, 4:5), safe zones, and effective sound design.



• Integrate Copy & CTAs: Seamlessly weave in primary text concepts, headlines, and clear calls-to-action as guided by the strategist.



• Collaborate Closely: Maintain clear, fast communication (Slack, etc.) with the Creative Strategist to receive feedback, discuss edits, and ensure alignment with performance goals.



• Asset Management: Organize and manage video files and project assets efficiently.



• Meet Deadlines: Deliver high-quality edits reliably and on schedule in a fast-paced environment.



Who We're Looking For:



• Proven Social Ad Editing Experience: Minimum 2+ experience specifically editing short-form video advertisements for performance marketing campaigns on Meta & TikTok. A portfolio showcasing direct-response style video ads is required.



• Technical Proficiency: Highly skilled in video editing software (e.g., Adobe Premiere Pro, Final Cut Pro, CapCut is a major plus). Proficiency in basic motion graphics (e.g., After Effects for text overlays) is desirable.



• Platform Native: Deep understanding of what works visually on TikTok and Meta feeds/stories/reels – you know the trends, the hooks, the pacing, the sounds that grab attention immediately.



• Performance-Oriented Mindset: You understand the goal is conversion, not just aesthetics. You are receptive to feedback driven by ad metrics and can adapt edits accordingly. (Links to Strategist's "Data Mastery," "Creativity That Converts").



• Speed and Agility: Ability to work quickly, execute variations efficiently, and adapt to changing requirements without sacrificing quality. (Links to Strategist's "Adaptability & Agility").



• Collaborative Spirit: Excellent communication skills and ability to work effectively and receive direction from a Creative Strategist. (Links to Strategist's "Collaboration is Key," "Clear Communication").



• Strong Work Ethic & Accountability: Reliable, proactive, meets deadlines, and takes ownership of edit quality – no babysitting needed. (Links to Strategist's "Work Ethic").



• Detail-Oriented: Ensures brand consistency, correct aspect ratios, and error-free text/graphics. Nothing sloppy makes the cut.



• Basic understanding of direct response marketing principles.



• Experience editing UGC content.



Compensation:

$1,000 base monthly salary, plus performance-based bonus/incentives tied to creative contribution.



How to Apply:



In your proposal, please include:



1. Provide your Resume/CV and link to your portfolio showcasing performance-driven social video ads for Meta/TikTok.

2. Briefly describe your process for editing a typical 15-45 sec. video ad.

3. How do you handle feedback and rapid iteration requests from creative strategists?

4. List your primary editing software and mention your experience with CapCut.

5. What AI tools or software have you used to create video ads? Provide as much detail as possible and share any previous AI work if any.

title: Assistant Property Manager
description:
INTRODUCTION: Hello! 
 (on IG). I'm an entrepreneur that started by buying rentals and is now developing houses by building and renovating in Nashville, TN. Now with ~35 units, I established a property management company (
) to manage them. My goal is 100 rental units, but to get there I need your help! I rely heavily on my team in place to keep things running smoothly so I can grow the company.



Location: Remote

Time Schedule: Business hours M - F in the U.S.A. (Overnight hours in the Philippines)

Compensation: $6-8/hr USD, paid bi-weekly via Onlinejobs.ph

Benefits: Paid USA bank holidays (~13 days) after trial period.



Job Summary:

Flywheel needs an APM to run the day to day business operations. You will report to me, Allan, for confirming outcome metrics and overcoming obstacles. You are the singular employee of Flywheel and play a critical role in its success!



The Perks:

• Play a direct role in a growing local company and enjoy a distinct sense of satisfaction from seeing results that YOU made happen.

• Enjoy autonomy of identifying and handling tasks using your own judgment, without micromanagement of a supervisor.

• Plenty of training provided to build on your existing experience and ensure your success!



A Typical Week:

• Receive notice of a tenant not renewing lease. Schedule contractor to begin turnover repairs.

• Receive a maintenance request from a unit in the software, and forward to the appropriate vendor.

• Follow up on certain tenants if rent not received on the 1st.

• Run month-end reports and distribute to rental property owners.



Qualifications:

• Read sections “Outcomes” & “Core Competencies” down below.

• This job is not for a “first timer”. Only those with Property Management experience need apply.

• Level of Completed Education: N/A

• English Fluency: 4+ out of 5. Some accent is fine but must be easy to understand.



===============================================



Mission: To manage a rental property portfolio for maximum profitability, especially by turning vacancies quickly and handling repairs and maintenance requests quickly and cost-effectively.



Outcomes: Property Management

• Rent collections are at 100% by the end of each month (excluding detainer warrants).

• Vacant units are filled in less than 3 weeks by getting units rent-ready, listing them, and screening tenants.

• Global Vacancy stays at or below 5% on a trailing 30 day basis (different from the above in that low global vacancy means tenants are staying and renewing leases likely due to effective management)

• All valid maintenance requests are completed within 7 days by quickly scheduling vendors and following up.

• Recurring tasks are performed on time by completing each item in the SOP’s checklists (Ex: Interim inspections, monthly owner statements and distributions).

• <24 hour response time on all property management-related
s, calls, & SMS.



Core Competencies

• Honesty & Integrity – Displays consistent, straightforward behavior. Always speaking in truth and not deception. Does not cut corners ethically.

• Proactive problem solving – Tackles new problems using self-administered resources.

• Own it – Sees tasks through to results (AKA tenacity or persistence)

• Assertiveness – Quick to take action, not afraid of conflict.

• Checklist Aficionado - Thorough executor of to-do lists and SOPs.

• Professional Communication skills – Concise and clear via phone and written. Grammar is largely appropriate.

• Intelligence –Retains information and thinks through tasks for optimum results at an above-average capability.

• High Attention to Detail – Does not let details slip through the cracks by carefully keeping track of each one.

• Work Ethic – Has no qualms about working many hours every day without dopamine distractions.

• Computer Literate – Is comfortable with computer-based web applications.

• Customer Service – Responds quickly to inquiries. Empathizing and moving them towards the goal.



TO APPLY: If you’ve read this far, show your attention to detail in your application: Add “FLYWHEEL” in all caps to the subject line (or title). Attach your resume and for a cover letter, use the application body to summarize your property management experience!

title: Director of Operations/Lead Manager
description:
Job Title: Virtual Administrative Assistant (Real Estate Focus)

 Location: Remote

 Hours: Full-Time | 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM EST (Monday to Friday)

 Compensation: Based on experience

About the Role:

We are seeking a highly organized and motivated Virtual Administrative Assistant to support our real estate operations. The ideal candidate will have a strong background in administrative tasks, real estate transaction coordination, CRM/lead management, and cold calling. This is a remote position that requires excellent communication skills, tech savviness, and the ability to multitask efficiently.



Key Responsibilities:

Real Estate Transaction Management





Coordinate all aspects of real estate transactions from contract to close





Communicate with clients, agents, escrow/title, lenders, and other stakeholders





Ensure all documentation is completed, filed, and compliant with real estate regulations





Lead & CRM Management





Manage and update lead databases (e.g., follow-up systems, pipeline tracking)





Organize and assign leads based on priority and agent availability





Monitor lead progress and provide weekly reports on conversion metrics





Administrative Support





Handle scheduling,
 management, calendar coordination, and data entry





Create and maintain spreadsheets and reports using Microsoft Excel





Utilize Google Workspace (Docs, Sheets, Drive, Calendar, Gmail) for document management and collaboration





Cold Calling & Client Outreach





Make outbound calls to prospective leads and set appointments





Follow scripts and maintain a professional tone with all contacts





Track outreach activity and update CRM systems accordingly







Qualifications:

Proven experience in real estate administration, transaction coordination, or a similar virtual assistant role





Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and Google Business Suite (Workspace)





Experience with CRM systems and lead management tools (e.g., Follow Up Boss, Zoho, Salesforce)





Strong written and verbal communication skills





Ability to work independently and meet deadlines in a fast-paced environment





Comfortable making cold calls and handling client objections





High attention to detail and problem-solving abilities







Preferred Qualifications:

Familiarity with U.S. real estate practices and documentation





Experience working in a real estate team environment





Bilingual (English/Spanish) is a plus but not required







To Apply:

Please submit your resume, a DISC report taken in the last 6 months along with a short cover letter detailing your relevant experience and why you’d be a great fit for this role.

title: Senior Bookkeeper
description:
Key Responsibilities



Bookkeeping & Financial Operations

*Manage full-cycle bookkeeping (A/R, A/P, general ledger, reconciliations)

*Maintain accurate and up-to-date financial records in QuickBooks

*Process payroll and ensure accuracy of employee compensation records

*Manage invoicing, collections, and vendor payments

*Perform monthly bank and credit card reconciliations

*Support tax preparation by maintaining organized financial documentation



Financial Reporting & Analysis

*Prepare and maintain weekly and monthly financial reports

*Build and manage Excel-based reports and dashboards to track performance

*Analyze variances in revenue, expenses, and key financial metrics

*Track KPIs and provide clear summaries of financial performance

*Assist leadership in understanding financial trends and business health



Data Integrity & Process Improvement

*Ensure accuracy and consistency across financial data and reporting systems

*Identify discrepancies and resolve issues proactively

*Improve reporting processes and streamline financial workflows

*Collaborate with leadership to improve visibility into financial performance



Required Experience & Skills

*3+ years of experience in bookkeeping, accounting, or financial operations is required

*Strong experience with QuickBooks is required.

*Advanced Excel skills (pivot tables, formulas, data analysis) is required

*Experience managing A/R, A/P, payroll, and reconciliations is required

*Strong attention to detail and high level of accuracy

*Ability to organize and present financial data clearly

*Experience with doing financial auditing is a huge plus

*Experience supporting US-based businesses is a plus





TECH REQUIREMENTS

Computer - at least i5 or equivalent with 8gb RAM

Internet Speed - At least 50 MBPS Download speed





Job Type: Full-time

Schedule: 1PM-9PM PST M-F / 4AM - 12 PM PHT Th-S

Salary Range: P60,000-80,000 monthly, Depending on experience



Application Instructions:

Fill out this form: 
/appqNAvkEQ10YFlc3/pagrQaNmvFaEJ5lG6/form

After submitting the form, click the apply button and send us a short message on why you would be a good fit for the role.

title: Real Estate Transaction Coordinator / Listing Coordinator (4)
description:
Why partner with us? WE ARE NOT YOUR ORDINARY CLIENT! (aka #NotYourOrdinaryAFAM lol) 



What is it like to work with us? HEAR FROM OUR TEAM! 



We’re hiring a highly organized, systems-minded Real Estate Transaction Coordinator.

This role is for someone who lives in the details, obsesses over contract deadlines, and can manage a complex transaction pipeline without a single ball being dropped. You aren't just "checking boxes"—you proactively identifies gaps in the contract-to-close process, automates repetitive tasks, and ensures our clients and partners experience a seamless, "magic 
nt" closing every single time.



IMPORTANT: Please read the entire job listing carefully. The final step of the hiring process will require the candidate to provide at least 1 former employer reference and at least 1 character reference. These will be verified prior to extending an offer.



ABOUT THE TEAM

Want to work in a rewarding, fast-paced, start-up atmosphere? Looking for significant growth and performance bonus opportunities? Have a growth mindset, value relationships, and are dedicated to getting results? Then we might be a fit!



Our Core Values:



DELIGHT THE CLIENT: We consistently exceed expectations by anticipating needs, delivering outstanding service, and creating those “magic 
nts” where every client feels seen and heard.



MASTER YOUR CRAFT: We commit to continuous learning, share our expertise openly, and tackle every challenge with precision and a deep sense of duty.



FOLLOW THROUGH, ALWAYS: When we say something will happen, it does—every time. We own every task, ensuring that nothing falls through the cracks, and when mistakes occur, we learn and improve immediately.

We are building an exceptional place to work with strong systems and high standards, not just managing tasks for a real estate business.



ABOUT YOU

Do you think in priorities, timelines, and dependencies? Do you enjoy turning a ratified purchase contract or listing agreements into a structured project plan with clear deadlines? Are you the type of person who catches a missing signature or a math error on a Closing Disclosure (CD) before anyone else sees it? Do you take pride in building clean systems, documenting SOPs, and keeping a team aligned without needing constant supervision? Do you love to communicate quickly and help keep the project on track?

If you are a high-level operator who wants to own the "engine room" of a high-volume real estate team, keep reading.



RESPONSIBILITIES

Transaction & Listing Management

Contract-to-Close Ownership: Manage every aspect of buyer and seller transactions from signed purchase agreement to closing using Skyslope and GoHighLevel.

Offer Packet Coordination: Prepare an offer packet draft with precise signature blocks and routing, and submit to the broker for review. 



Compliance & Auditing: Submit all required documentation to the office broker; ensure 100% file compliance at direction of the responsible broker by monitoring for no missing disclosures.

Closing Logistics: Coordinate with Attorneys, Lenders, Title officers and brokers; collect CD/ALTA statements for broker accuracy review against the commission agreement.



Project & Operations Management

System Building: Spot inefficiencies in the workflow and design/implement improvements or automations to reduce manual effort.



SOP Documentation: Document workflows and create step-by-step guides so the process is repeatable and scalable.

KPI Reporting: Maintain and update the "Deal Tracker" G-Sheet and GHL Opportunity values to ensure leadership has accurate data at all times.



The "Connective Tissue": Act as the bridge between leadership, agents, and clients to ensure no ball is ever dropped.



REQUIREMENTS



Experience & Capability



1. Must have extensive experience in real estate transaction coordination, transaction management, or as a high-level operations associate.

2. Preferred BPO Experience: Previous background in BPO Customer Service is highly preferred.

3. Project Management Experience: Comfortable managing 20+ active files simultaneously with varying deadlines.

4. Automation Mindset: Experience automating workflows (or a deep understanding of how to design them) is highly preferred.

5. Technical Proficiency: Working knowledge of Skyslope and GoHighLevel (GHL) is strongly preferred but not required. Fluent in AI tools (Gemini/Claude/OpenClaw) to improve output speed.



Work Style & Standards

Extremely Detail-Oriented: You find the needle in the haystack every time.

Near-Native English: Exceptional written and spoken communication skills.

Proactive Ownership: You don’t wait to be told something is broken; you anticipate it and fix it before it breaks.

Speed & Urgency: You move with a sense of purpose and meet every deadline, no excuses.



Technical Requirements

A STABLE, HIGH-SPEED INTERNET CONNECTION is required (at least 150mbps).

Must be available to respond to messages within a 12-hour daily window (standard core hours 8:00 AM – 8:00 PM EST).

Must be flexible to work 2–4 hours on weekends as needed for urgent business requirements



APPLICATION & RECRUITMENT PROCESS



STEP 1: Application

Please submit your application HERE 

To ensure you have read through the requirements, please write the phrase "green kangaroo" in the Additional Comments field of the application.



STEP 2: Initial Interview

If your application indicates you might be a great fit, you will be invited to participate in a quick, 10- to 15-minute initial screening interview.



STEP 3: Case Interview

Candidates who pass the initial screening will be invited to a 1-hour case interview. A case interview is an interactive session where we present you with a real-world scenario or business challenge relevant to the role. Rather than just talking about your past experience, this allows us to see your problem-solving process, technical logic, and how you tackle practical situations in real-time.



STEP 4: Reference Check

This is the final step before an official offer is extended. The final candidate(s) must provide one former employer/lead reference and one character reference.



STEP 5: The 90-Day Trial Period

If an offer is made and accepted, your employment will begin with a 90-day PAID trial period. This period is dedicated to ensuring a strong mutual fit—both for the responsibilities of the role and alignment with our core values. 

During these 90 days, we will provide you with all the necessary training, onboarding, and support you need to succeed and thrive on our team. You will be expected to master the Skyslope/GHL workflow, meet all transaction deadlines, and begin documenting/improving our internal systems. A successful trial results in a permanent invitation to the team.



Please submit your application HERE >>

title: EXPERIENCED SHORT FORM VIDEO EDITOR | Flexi Schedule
description:
We’re hiring someone who knows how to turn long-form content into sharp, high-retention short videos for platforms like TikTok, Instagram Reels, and YouTube Shorts.



This role is for someone who understands pacing, hooks, and visual rhythm in short-form content, and knows how to edit with intention rather than just cutting clips.



—-----------------------------------------------------------------------------



READ THE ENTIRE JOB LISTING CAREFULLY. The final step of the hiring process will require the candidate to provide at least 1 former employer/team lead reference and at least 1 character reference. These will be verified prior to extending an offer.



--- ABOUT THE TEAM ---

Want to work in a rewarding, fast-paced, start-up atmosphere? Looking for significant growth and performance bonus opportunities? Have a growth mindset, value relationships, and dedicated to getting results? Then we might be a fit!



What is it like to work with us? HEAR FROM OUR TEAM!   





Our Core Values:

1) DELIGHT THE CLIENT:  We consistently exceed expectations by anticipating needs, delivering outstanding service, and creating those “magic 
nts” where every client feels seen and heard.

2) MASTER YOUR CRAFT:  We commit to continuous learning, share our expertise openly, and tackle every challenge with precision and a deep sense of duty.

3) FOLLOW THROUGH, ALWAYS:  When we say something will happen, it does—every time. We own every task, ensuring that nothing falls through the cracks, and when mistakes occur, we learn and improve immediately.



Our core values are important and the right candidate will share our values. We are building an awesome place to work, not just cutting together videos for our real estate business.



--- ABOUT YOU ---

Do you have strong creative instincts and know how to spot 
nts that make people stop scrolling? Are you proactive, adaptable, and detail-oriented? Do you have a sharp sense of pacing, an instinct for hooks, and a clear understanding of how short-form videos need to flow to hold attention? Do you take pride in clean edits and delivering videos that feel intentional and finished? Do our core values sound like a fit for who you are?



Then keep reading to see if we're a fit!



Responsibilities:

Short-Form Editing & Story Selection

- Review long-form videos, transcripts, or raw footage to identify short-form clip opportunities

- Select 
nts that are clear, engaging, and platform-appropriate

- Edit vertical videos optimized for TikTok, Instagram Reels, and YouTube Shorts

- Create strong hooks, clean cuts, and natural pacing

- Deliver publish-ready videos that meet brand and platform standards



Captions, Visuals & Formatting

- Add on-screen captions (burned-in or SRT as required) with proper timing and readability

- Apply basic motion, zooms, punch-ins, and visual emphasis where appropriate

- Follow platform-specific formatting (aspect ratio, duration, safe margins)



Iteration & Workflow

- Implement feedback efficiently and meet agreed turnaround times

- Maintain clear file naming and folder organization

- Track edits, revisions, and delivery status as required



Requirements:

- A STABLE, HIGH-SPEED INTERNET CONNECTION, REQUIRED (at least 150mbps)

- Experience with Final Cut Pro X (or other editing software, such as Adobe After Effects and Premiere Pro) is required

- Strong preference for MacOS users

- Experience editing short-form vertical content for social platforms, required

- Strong understanding of short-form pacing, hooks, and retention

- Experience working from transcripts or long-form content, required

- Clean file organization and understanding of post-production workflow

- Portfolio of completed short-form video projects, required

- General understanding of brand, content marketing, YouTube, etc, required

- GREAT interpersonal and communication skills, required

- Ability to self-start, work on a tight schedule, and manage multiple project deadlines, required

- Ability to follow directions and a LOVE of completing lots of projects according to consistent specifications, required

- Growth mindset, required



--- APPLICATION & RECRUITMENT PROCESS ---

STEP 1:

Fill out the application form through the link below and write "green kangaroo" in the Additional Comments field.


 



STEP 2:

Qualified candidates may be invited to participate in two PAID test projects, $10-20 USD for each test project estimated to be 2-3hrs of work for each (a total of $40 USD!).



STEP 3:

Candidates who advance through the test project rounds will be invited to an interview.



STEP 4:

Before extending a trial offer, we will conduct a CHARACTER REFERENCE CHECK for the final candidate(s). CONDUCTING A REFERENCE CHECK will be the last step in the hiring process.



STEP 5:

Upon receiving a trial offer, you will be onboarded as a Video Editor with a 90-day PAID trial period. During this period, we expect you to demonstrate your video editing expertise, meet project deadlines, be a team player, and excel at what you do. A successful trial will lead to a permanent offer to join the team.

title: Senior GoHighLevel Specialist | CRM Admin, Funnel Builder, Systems Design, HighLevel
description:
Would YOU LOVE to work here? SEE WHAT OUR TEAM SAYS!

WATCH TEAM VIDEO: youtu.be/smT-w2M_TcQ



PLEASE READ ALL INSTRUCTIONS AND DESCRIPTION CAREFULLY BEFORE YOU APPLY!

Before we offer you a job, we will ask for people who know you (like an old boss or colleague) to tell us about you. We will check these before you can get a job offer.



WE ARE LOOKING FOR SOMEONE WHO:



- Pays close attention to ALL the little details in complex systems and campaign setups.

- Likes to build and optimize processes the RIGHT way, following all the steps for effective automation.

- Has reliable home internet. You'll be accessing our systems daily, though file sizes are generally small.

- Is a pro at building automations, connecting different tools, and figuring out how to make our GoHighLevel setup simpler and more effective, including recommending the best tools for the job.

- Understands how to design, build, manage, and improve our systems and processes.

- Knows GoHighLevel inside and out. You are an expert and a go-to problem solver!

- Is great at learning new GoHighLevel-related and similar funnel-building tools, and is happy to research and learn new tools as needed.

- MUST LOVE marketing automation, building efficient systems, and have a portfolio of GoHighLevel projects or campaigns you can share.

- Can tackle complex GoHighLevel tasks (like a test project we give you) pretty fast AND carefully. Doing things quickly and correctly is very important!

- Has a flexible work schedule but can be available for support during key times, like when we launch new automations or features.

--- ABOUT OUR TEAM ---



Want to work in a fun, exciting place?

Want to learn a lot, get paid fairly so you don't have to take on extra clients just to get by, and have the chance to earn bonuses if you do great?

Do you like to learn new skills, get along well with other people on your team (who are very cool people), and take pride that you finish your work on time (or early)?



This could be the place you have been dreaming of!



CORE VALUES:



DELIGHT THE CLIENT: We always try to do more than clients ask. We want every client to feel special and happy with our work and the systems we build.

MASTER YOUR CRAFT: We always try to learn new things about GoHighLevel and marketing automation. We share what we know and do every job carefully and well.

FOLLOW THROUGH, ALWAYS: If we say we will do something, we do it every time. We take care of all our tasks. If we make a mistake, we fix it and learn from it.

We want someone who likes these ideas. We are building a great team, not just managing marketing campaigns for our real estate business.



--- ABOUT YOU (OUR FUTURE SENIOR GOHIGHLEVEL SPECIALIST!) ---



Are you careful and good with details, especially in CRM and automation platforms?

Do you like designing and implementing marketing systems that run smoothly?

Do you love creating powerful automations and sales funnels using GoHighLevel?

Do you know what makes a marketing campaign effective and how to track its success?

Do you want to help bring ambitious marketing strategies to life?

Do our Core Values sound good to you?



Then keep reading!



WHAT YOU WILL DO (YOUR RESPONSIBILITIES AS A SENIOR GOHIGHLEVEL SPECIALIST):



(Your main job will be to manage, optimize, and scale our marketing efforts using GoHighLevel. You will be the lead specialist for our GHL setup, ensuring it supports our business goals effectively by designing, implementing, maintaining, troubleshooting, and improving systems.)



DESIGN & IMPLEMENT GOHIGHLEVEL STRATEGIES:



Develop and build out comprehensive sales funnels, marketing automation sequences, and lead nurturing campaigns within GoHighLevel.

Configure and customize GoHighLevel accounts, including pipelines, calendars, triggers, workflows, and third-party integrations.

Manage and segment our contact database within GHL, ensuring data hygiene and effective targeting.

Advise on and implement best practices for GHL setup, potentially simplifying existing implementations or integrating third-party tools where beneficial.

OPTIMIZE & MAINTAIN SYSTEMS:



Monitor the performance of GHL campaigns and automations, identifying areas for improvement and implementing A/B tests.

Troubleshoot and resolve any technical issues or bugs within the GoHighLevel platform and connected tools.

Stay up-to-date with the latest GoHighLevel features and best practices, implementing them to enhance our marketing efforts.

Continuously look for ways to improve processes and system efficiency.

SUPPORT & COLLABORATE:



Create reports and dashboards to track key marketing metrics and provide insights to the team.

Provide expert-level support and guidance to tea
mbers on GoHighLevel functionalities and best practices.

Collaborate with the marketing team and other stakeholders to align GHL strategies with overall business objectives.

Document processes, workflows, and campaign setups within GoHighLevel.

WHAT YOU NEED (REQUIREMENTS):



GOHIGHLEVEL EXPERTISE:



Proven, in-depth experience managing and building complex setups in GoHighLevel. You should be able to demonstrate advanced proficiency. GoHighLevel certifications are a huge plus.

A portfolio showcasing successful GoHighLevel projects, funnels, or campaigns you have designed, built, or significantly managed.

Strong understanding of CRM principles, marketing automation best practices, sales funnel psychology, digital marketing analytics, and process design/improvement.

Demonstrated ability to understand how different tools integrate and how to build robust, interconnected automations.

WORK STYLE AND COMMUNICATION:



Good at talking, listening, problem-solving, and working as part of a team.

Able to follow strategic direction while also proactively identifying opportunities and working independently.

Highly organized and able to manage your time effectively to meet deadlines and manage multiple projects.

Eager to learn new things and get better at your job (you want to MASTER YOUR CRAFT in GoHighLevel and related technologies).

It helps if you understand how marketing automation directly impacts business growth and client acquisition.

Some understanding (or moderate skill) of copywriting is a huge bonus, but not required.

Some understanding of or passion for real estate is a plus, but not essential.

--- HOW TO APPLY (OUR HIRING STEPS) ---



STEP 1:

Type 'green kangaroo' in the application subject line so we know you read this.



STEP 2:

If we think you might be a good fit, we’ll ask you to do up to three small, PAID test projects. Each one pays $15 (USD) and should take about 1 to 2 hours to complete. (That's up to $45 USD total!)



STEP 3:

If you do well on the test projects, we’ll invite you for an interview to talk more about your experience and approach.



STEP 4:

Before we offer a trial job, we’ll talk to people who know you (your references), particularly those who can speak to your GoHighLevel expertise.



STEP 5:

If all goes well, we’ll offer you a 90-day PAID trial job as a Senior GoHighLevel Specialist. This is your chance to show us your skills, work with the team, and do a great job. If the trial period is a success, we’ll offer you a permanent job on the team! ALL our permanent tea
mbers have been with us for almost 3 years and counting, and we are very proud of that!

title: CPA / Financial Advisor - Government Contracting / Real Estate / Multi-LLC
description:
CPA / Financial Advisor



Part-Time (10–20 hrs/mo)  |  Remote — Philippines-Based  |  CPA Required  |  Government Contracting Experience Strongly Preferred





About You --------------------



You're not a bookkeeper who got promoted. You're a credentialed financial professional who understands the unique complexity of U.S. government contracting — and you're comfortable advising a CEO who moves fast and expects you to keep up.



You've reviewed sets of books and immediately known what's right, what's off, and what's a risk. You understand indirect rates, FAR cost principles, and what it takes to survive a DCAA audit. You can build a budget for a government proposal, review a month-end close, and explain cash flow implications to an executive in plain language — all in the same engagement.



You bring the kind of financial judgment that a costly U.S. CPA would provide — and you're available, responsive, and invested in the client's long-term success. If that's you, we'd like to talk.





About Performance Excellence Partners  --------------------



Performance Excellence Partners (PEP) is a multimillion-dollar, woman-owned government contracting agency serving federal clients across the United States. CEO Rachel Ramirez leads a growing organization that also includes a real estate portfolio (multiple LLCs), a coaching and leadership development practice, and nonprofit work. The books are clean. The team is capable. What's needed is a trusted financial advisor who can provide oversight, strategic counsel, and the expertise to take PEP's financial operations to the next level.





About the Role --------------------



This is a fractional CPA/Financial Advisor engagement — approximately 10–20 hours per month, with potential for more during proposal season, audit periods, or major financial decisions.



You will work alongside PEP's Senior Bookkeeper (who handles day-to-day operations) and report directly to Rachel. Your job is to review, advise, and protect — not to process transactions or manage the books yourself.



Once the financial systems are well-established and the bookkeeper is running smoothly, Rachel's expectation is that this engagement may settle to approximately 2–3 hours of review and advisory per month — the mark of a highly efficient, well-structured financial operation.



What You'll Do ---------------------------------



Monthly Oversight



Review the bookkeeper's monthly close package — entries, reconciliations, and financial reports

Verify job costing and contract billing accuracy (Book to Bill)

Identify errors, misclassifications, or compliance risks and provide corrective guidance

Conduct a monthly advisory meeting with Rachel — financial review, insights, and recommendations



Strategic Financial Advisory



Develop and maintain annual operating budget and multi-entity financial plan

Support government proposal budget development — ensure bids are sound, competitive, and compliant

Update provisional billing rates and forward pricing rate proposals as needed

Scenario modeling for major financial decisions (new contracts, headcount, acquisitions)

Advise on tax strategy across PEP and all affiliated entities



Compliance & Audit Readiness



Design and maintain internal controls appropriate for a FAR-governed government contractor

Ensure books are structured and documented to withstand DCAA scrutiny

Review corporate certification financial packages for accuracy and compliance

Oversee year-end financial package delivery to tax accountant



Loans, Applications & Personal Financial Support



Review and validate financial data for business loans and government applications

Prepare or review personal wealth statements for Rachel

Support financial sections of RFPs, RFIs, and capability statements

Advise on personal financial matters as they relate to Rachel's business decisions





A Day in the Life --------------------



It's the first week of the month. You log in, review the bookkeeper's close package for the prior month, and make notes on two entries that need reclassification and one contract invoice where the billed amount doesn't align with the task order ceiling.



You prepare a brief review memo — three findings, one of which is a compliance risk, two of which are clean-up items — and send it to the bookkeeper with clear guidance. Then you join a 90-minute call with Rachel to review the month's financials, discuss Q3 cash flow, and walk through a preliminary budget for an upcoming federal proposal.



Rachel has a question about whether she should structure a new property acquisition under an existing LLC or create a new one. You walk her through the tax implications and liability considerations in plain language and give her a clear recommendation. That's the whole month — efficient, high-value, and exactly what Rachel needs.





What We're Looking For  --------------------



Philippine CPA license required; U.S. CPA or equivalent credential a significant differentiator

Minimum 7 years of accounting experience, with meaningful government contracting exposure

Direct knowledge of FAR cost principles and DCAA audit requirements

Experience with indirect rate structure, provisional billing rates, and forward pricing

Multi-entity accounting experience — real estate LLCs a strong advantage

Prior experience advising U.S.-based clients, ideally in a fractional or outsourced capacity

QuickBooks proficiency (Desktop and Online) — must be able to review and navigate the books

Excellent English communication — can explain complex financial matters clearly to a non-finance audience

Proactive, confident advisor — not a passive reporter





Schedule & Compensation  --------------------



Part-time fractional engagement: estimated 10–20 hours per month

Flexible scheduling with availability for a recurring monthly review call (U.S. Pacific Time)

Compensation: $18–$25 USD/hour based on credentials and experience

Paid monthly via Wise or direct bank transfer

Potential to expand engagement scope as PEP grows





To Apply  --------------------



Please apply here:  



Thank you for taking the time to help us get to know you.

title: Digital Marketing Manager/Strategist (remote)
description:
AM Eyewear, Australia’s Longest-Running Independent Fashion Eyewear Brand, Established in 2003.

We’re looking for a highly skilled Digital Marketing Manager/Strategist to join AM Eyewear part time and help drive the next stage of our growth. If you live and breathe digital, love fashion, and want to work with a brand that actually values premium design and smart strategy, this one’s for you. (THIS IS NOT A SOCIAL MEDIA MANAGEMENT ROLE)

This role sits at the centre of our marketing engine, leading strategy and coordinating our Meta, Google Ads, SEO and Klaviyo specialists, managing campaigns end-to-end, and keeping everything aligned with performance goals and brand standards.

This position begins at 10 hours per week, with an hourly rate ranging from PHP 600 to 1,200. We offer regular performance reviews, with the opportunity to adjust the rate in line with demonstrated results.

Agencies welcome or if you currently work at an agency and want to take on a premium client after hours, we’d love to hear from you.

What You’ll Be Doing

• Oversee and execute AM Eyewear’s digital marketing strategy across Meta, Google, Klaviyo and other key channels.

• Liaise with our SEO team, as well as our Meta and Google Ads media buyers and Klaviyo specialist to plan, coordinate and optimise campaigns.

• Brief creatives, campaign angles and performance insights to our design team.

• Manage the rollout of new product pushes, seasonal campaigns, and brand launches.

• Analyse performance data, prepare weekly reports for the Founder, and recommend improvements.

• Keep a close eye on trends in fashion, luxury, and content to ensure we’re always culturally on-point.

• Maintain strict brand consistency across digital channels.

What We’re Looking For

• 3+ years experience in digital marketing (e-commerce preferred).

• Strong understanding of Meta Ads, Google Ads, Klaviyo, CRO, and analytics.

• Someone who can think strategically, not just execute tasks.

• Excellent communication skills, you’ll be coordinating multiple specialists.

• A natural feel for premium aesthetics, fashion culture and luxury brand behaviour.

• Reliable, proactive and organised.

• Comfortable working autonomously and reporting directly to the Founder.

Nice to Have

• Experience working with eyewear, fashion, luxury or lifestyle brands.

• Basic design or content editing skills.

• Strong copywriting instincts.

 

What You’ll Love

• Working with a premium global eyewear brand known for quality, artistry and innovation.

• Flexible hours, ideal if you currently work at an agency.

• Direct access to the Founder, fast decisions, no corporate red tape.

• Long-term role with room to expand your responsibilities as the brand grows.

How to Apply

Send your CV, portfolio/case studies, and a short note on why you want to work with AM Eyewear to 
 Please note, this role is only for candidates with extensive Digital Marketing Knowledge and all other applicants will not be responded to.

title: Flutter Frontend Developer w/ Game Dev Experience
description:
About RAVIA



RAVIA is a location-based AR edutainment mobile app for children aged 6–17, built for iOS and Android. Kids explore real-world geofenced locations to collect AR tokens, complete daily puzzles, and upgrade their avatar — a Rav — to unlock real-world partner rewards.



The app is built on a Flutter + Unity architecture: Flutter owns the UI shell, navigation, and all interactive puzzle experiences, while Unity drives the AR layer. Puzzles are delivered as web-based mini-games inside a Flutter WebView, giving us full flexibility over game design without bloating the native app.



We're a small, focused team. The work is meaningful, the tech is interesting, and the product is genuinely fun to build.



The Role



We're looking for a Flutter developer who can also build web-based games — someone who is equally comfortable writing clean Dart as they are building an interactive HTML5 puzzle that runs inside a mobile WebView. This is not a generalist role; we need someone with real hands-on experience in both areas.



You will work directly on the Flutter app and own the delivery of the puzzle game layer — designing, building, and integrating six puzzle types (Memory, Wordle, Arithmetic, Sudoku, Crossword, Spaceman) as web games rendered inside a Flutter WebView, with full age-adaptive difficulty progression.



What You'll Be Doing

- Build and maintain the Flutter app across iOS and Android, translating Figma mockups into polished, performant UI

- Develop interactive web-based mini-games (HTML5/CSS/JS) that run inside a Flutter WebView for iOS and Android

- Architect and implement the WebView bridge — JavaScript channels, message passing between Dart and web game layer

- Implement all six puzzle types with age-gated difficulty matrices, auto-progression logic, and daily state management

- Integrate the Flutter–Unity communication bridge (platform channels) for the AR game layer

- Build accessibility-first UI: dyslexia-friendly fonts, adjustable contrast, large tap targets, and optional timed pressure toggle

- Integrate Flutter screens against a Node.js/PostgreSQL REST API

- Collaborate with our UI/UX designer, game designer, and backend developer to deliver a cohesive experience

- Write clean, maintainable code that a small team can build on over time



What We Need (Required)

- Strong Flutter/Dart skills — you've shipped a real iOS and Android app

- Solid HTML5, CSS, and JavaScript — you build things with it, not just describe them

- Hands-on experience building games or rich interactive experiences for the web (canvas API, Phaser, PixiJS, or equivalent)

- Experience integrating WebView inside a mobile app and communicating bidirectionally between native and web contexts

- Comfortable with REST API integration in Flutter (Dio, http, or similar)

- State management experience — Provider, Riverpod, Bloc, or equivalent

- Familiar with iOS and Android build, signing, and deployment pipelines



Bonus Points

- Experience with Flutter–Unity integration or flutter_unity_widget

- Experience building in Unity

- Background in children's apps, edutainment, or designing for younger audiences

- Familiarity with accessibility standards (WCAG, mobile a11y best practices)

- Exposure to location-based or AR mobile apps

- Experience with offline-first architecture and local data sync

- Comfortable working in a small startup-style team with evolving requirements



What This Role Is Not



This is not a backend role — we have a backend developer; you will consume APIs, not build them

This is not a design role — mockups are provided; your job is faithful, high-quality implementation



How to Apply



Send us a brief intro, your portfolio or relevant work samples (especially anything game-related or WebView-heavy), and your availability. We move quickly — no drawn-out hiring processes.

If you bring ideas, energy, and a strong creative instinct — we want to see it!

Bonus if you have experience with short-form content, Instagram, TikTok, YouTube Shorts, or AI tools like ChatGPT and CapCut.



How to Apply



Send a message with “CSM” as the subject line.



Include ONLY:



#1: Your CV

#2: Years of short-form content experience

#3: Years of client management experience

#4: One sentence explaining why you are a strong fit

#5: Your portfolio



Applications that do not follow this format will not be reviewed.



P.S. Recruitment is managed by Young Publisher. Successful candidates will work under Social Influence Consulting LLC.

title: Funnel Specialist — GoHighLevel Systems & QA
description:
Application Link: 



Who we are



Grow Baby Grow is a marketing automation agency that builds and manages CRM systems, funnels, and automation workflows for home service and local businesses. Our stack is GoHighLevel, Zapier, Asana, and Slack, plus a set of internal QA processes we've built over time.



Everything our clients depend on — lead capture, follow-up sequences, CRM pipelines, appointment booking — runs through systems we build and maintain. When those systems break, clients lose leads. Our job is to make sure they don't break.



The role



This is a systems and operations role, not a creative marketing role.



You'll own funnel health across multiple client accounts. That means building new funnels, auditing existing ones on a weekly cadence, diagnosing failures by tracing trigger logic (not guessing), and documenting every change you make. 



You'll use our internal quality system called TAO — Tracking, Auditing, Optimization — to catch errors before clients see them.



What you'll do day to day



- Build and configure GoHighLevel funnels: landing pages, forms, pipelines, chatbots, automation workflows

- Run weekly TAO audits — systematic checks on triggers, workflow steps, messaging, integrations, and pipeline logic

- Diagnose broken automations by tracing the full trigger chain, finding the failure point, fixing it, and documenting what happened

- Launch new funnels and updates without breaking existing systems — pre-launch QA checklists, post-launch monitoring

- Document every funnel you build or touch — no undocumented changes

- Publish weekly stability reports using standardized scorecards

- Coordinate with the team async via Slack and Asana — flag problems early, don't wait to be asked



What we're looking for



- 2+ years in marketing automation, funnel ops, or CRM administration

- Hands-on GoHighLevel experience — you've built workflows, debugged triggers, managed pipelines in GHL specifically

- Zapier experience — building, debugging, and documenting multi-step integrations

- Experience auditing or QA-testing live systems

- Agency or client-services background preferred — you understand accountability to outcomes, not just task completion

- Strong English — you'll communicate daily with the team and occasionally with clients



You'll thrive here if



- You've inherited someone else's messy GHL account and turned it into something stable and documented

- You keep personal QA checklists without being told to

- You've been called "too thorough" and took it as a compliment

- You don't mark a task done until you've verified the output in the live system — not just built it, but confirmed it works

- You treat a weekly audit as pass/fail, not a suggestion



You won't thrive here if



- You troubleshoot by changing things until something works instead of tracing the logic

- You rely on daily direction to know what to do next

- You've used GHL casually but haven't built or maintained systems in it for paying clients



Tools

- GoHighLevel (primary — deep proficiency required), Zapier, Asana, Slack, Google Sheets/Docs, Loom



Compensation & benefits



- $1,200–$1,600/month on full time basis depending on experience

- 20 days of planned time off per year (no reduction in monthly pay)

- Quarterly performance bonus eligibility

- Fully remote — small team where your work has direct, visible impact on client results

- Growth path as the agency scales



How to apply

Fill out the application form:

nts (wins, reactions, tension)

this is building athlete brands through content.



If you’re someone who wants to:

We want to work with you.

title: AI-Powered Resume and Content VA
description:
The Opportunity

You will support the business owner directly, handling the tasks that keep the practice running day to day. This is not a traditional admin role. The owner uses custom ChatGPT prompts, Loom video training, and Google Docs to document every workflow so you are never left guessing. Execution, consistency, and proactive communication are what matter most here.

 

The owner is intentional about building this relationship over time. Start with clear, structured tasks, earn trust, and take on more autonomy as the partnership grows.



WHAT YOU WILL DO

Resume and Docs (30%)

Rewrite and format resumes, cover letters, and LinkedIn profiles using Kolby's prompts and custom GPTs

Process and organize client intake documents, coaching notes, and speaking engagement materials



Social Media (30%)

Draft, schedule, and manage LinkedIn and social media content following Kolby's content calendar and brand voice

Project Management (25%)

Manage and organize tasks in Asana: daily prioritization, project tracking, and deadline monitoring

Coordinate post-event follow-ups: send tailored
s to speaking engagement attendees based on survey responses

Submit daily and weekly activity reports tracking completed work, blockers, and priorities



Admin (15%)

Support B2B outreach to wealth management firms and outplacement prospects via LinkedIn and

Research companies with recent layoffs to identify outplacement partnership opportunities





WHAT WE ARE LOOKING FOR

AI proficiency: Comfortable using ChatGPT and custom GPTs daily; can coach AI output rather than copy-paste

Strong written English: Can edit AI-generated content to sound natural and remove robotic patterns

Project management: Experience with Asana or similar tools; can organize, prioritize, and track work independently

Social media savvy: Familiar with LinkedIn content best practices; can schedule and manage posts

Reliable and communicative: Consistent availability, stable internet, responsive within working hours

Self-starter: Can follow Loom walkthroughs, written SOPs, and Google Docs instructions with minimal hand-holding



Compensation and Schedule:

Monthly RateApproximately $1,200 to $1,500 USD

Hours Full-time, 40 hours per week

Schedule: Flexible async with preferred overlap starting at 2:00 PM Pacific (approximately 5:00 AM Philippine Standard Time) for 2 to 3 hours of daily communication

Start: ASAP



To Apply, send a cover letter and updated resume
